rudradhyaya.sanskrit:
Iॐ नमः परमात्मने ।

ऋग्भिः ' स्तुवन्ति यजुर्भिर्यजन्ति सामभिर्गायन्ति ' इत्युक्तेर्यजनार्थकस्य यजुर्वेदस्य
पञ्चमोऽयमध्यायः ' रुद्राध्याय ' इत्युच्यते । चतुर्थेऽध्याये सर्वा इष्टकाचितय उक्ताः ।
रुद्राध्यायसंज्ञकेऽस्मिन्पञ्चमेऽध्याये चित्याग्नौ होम उच्यते । अस्य च कर्मकाण्डे
पाठात्कर्माङ्गत्वं तथा च श्रुतिः—
    ' रुद्रो वा एष यदग्निः स एतर्हि जातो यर्हि सर्वश्रितः स यथा
    वत्सो जातः स्तनं प्रेप्सत्येवं वा एष एतर्हि भागधेयं प्रेप्सति
    तस्मै यदाहुतिं न जुहुयादध्वर्युं च यजमानं च ध्यायेच्छतरु-
    द्रीयं जुहोति भागधेयेनैवैनं शमयति नार्तिमार्च्छत्यध्वर्युर्न यजमानः ' .
इति । स्मृतिरपि—
    ' चमकं नमकं चैव पौरुषं सूक्तमेव च ।
    नित्यं त्रयं प्रयुञ्जानो ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ' ॥ इति ।
याज्ञवल्क्योऽपि—
    ' सुरापः स्वर्णहारी च रुद्रजापी जले स्थितः ।
    सहस्रशीर्षाजापी च मुच्यते सर्वकिल्बिषैः ' ॥

    इत्यादिभिरस्य कर्माङ्गत्वं प्रसिद्धमेव भवति । जावालोपनिषदि ज्ञानहेतुत्वमप्यस्य
श्रूयते । तथाच ब्रह्मचारिभिः 'किं जप्तेनामृतत्वमश्नुते?' इति पृष्टो याज्ञवल्क्य आह—
' शतरुद्रीयेण ' इति । ' एतानि ह वा अमृतस्य नामधेयानि भवन्ति एतैर्ह
वा अमृतो भवति ' इति कैवल्योपनिषदि—' यः शतरुद्रीयमधीते सोऽग्निपूतो
भवति । इत्यादिभिः । स्मृतयोऽपि—
    ' सततं रुद्रजाप्योऽसौ परां मुक्तिमवाप्स्यति ' ॥
इत्यादयो बह्व्यः । एवं कर्माङ्गत्वज्ञानहेतुत्वाभ्यां विषयिभिर्मुमुक्षुभिश्चावश्यमाद-
रणीयोऽयं रुद्राध्यायः । वर्तते चास्य सर्वत्र पठनपाठनप्रचारो विशेषतया दाक्षिणा-
त्येषु । यद्यपि केवलं पाठमात्रेणाप्ययमिष्टफलदो भवत्येव तथापि ।
    ' उत त्वः पश्यन् न ददर्श वाचमुत त्वः शृण्वन् शृणोत्येनाम् ।
    उतो त्वस्मै तन्वं विसस्रे जायेव पत्य उशती सुवासाः ' ॥
इत्यादिषु वेदार्थाभिज्ञस्य प्रशंसोक्तेः—
    ' यद्गृहीतमविज्ञातं निगदेनैव शब्द्यते ।
    अनग्नाविव शुष्कैधो न तज्ज्वलति कर्हिचित् ' ॥

( २ )

इत्यादिभिर्नर्थज्ञस्य निन्दाश्रवणात्—

' स्थाणुरयं भारहारः किलाभूद्धषील्य वेदं न विजानाति योऽथैमू ।
योऽधैज्ञ इत्सकलं मद्रमश्रुते नाकमेति ज्ञानविधूतपाप्मा ' ।।

इत्यादिभिर्योनिभिज्ञस्य स्थाणुत्वोक्त्या निन्दाया अर्थज्ञस्य च सकळकल्याणप्राप्तेर्-
मिधानादर्थज्ञानमत्यावश्यकमेव । स्थाणुरिति दष्टान्त: । छिन्नशाखं शुष्कं वृक्षमूर्छं
स्थाणुशब्देनोच्यते । केचित्त्वर्थमजानतोऽपि यथावत्पाठमात्रेणैव कर्माङ्कत्वं सिद्ध्य-
तीति मन्यन्ते । ' स्वाध्यायोऽध्येतव्यः ' इत्यस्याध्ययन विधेर्वेदाक्षरग्रहणेनैव चरि-
तार्थत्वम् । पूर्वकाण्डार्थज्ञानं कर्मानुष्ठानाक्षिप्तम् ।कर्मानुष्ठानं पाठमात्रेणैव भवतीति तत्र
सर्वसंमतम् । 'अनर्थज्ञोऽल्पकण्ठश्च पठेते पाठकाधमाः:' इत्यादिप्वनर्थज्ञस्य पाठका-
धमत्वोक्तेर्थज्ञानं विना पाठमात्रमपि न सम्यक्तया भवितुमर्हति । यद्यपि कर्मानुष्ठानं
कैवळेन मन्त्रपाठेनैव सिद्ध्यति तथाऽपि केवळानुष्ठानाद्विद्यासहितेऽनुष्ठाने फलातिशय-
मामनन्ति च्छन्दोगाः ' तेनोभौ कुरुतो यश्चैतदेवं वेद् यश्च न वेद् । नाना तु
विद्या चाविद्या च । यदेव विद्यया करोति श्रद्धयोपनिषदा तदेव वीर्यवत्तरं
भवति ' । अध्ययनं नामार्थज्ञानपूर्वकमक्षरग्रहणम् । तथा सत्यध्ययनविधेरर्थज्ञान-
पूर्वकमक्षरग्रहणेनैव चारितार्थ्यं भवेन् केवळमक्षरग्रहणेनैव । ज्ञानहेतुत्वं त्वर्थज्ञानं विना
न भवेदेव । आधुनिकानां तु सर्वेषामर्थज्ञानस्याऽऽवश्यकत्वे न काऽपि विप्रतिपत्तिः ।
तत्सिद्धं कर्माङ्कत्वेऽप्यस्य रुद्राध्यायस्यार्थग्रहणस्याऽऽवश्यकत्वम् ।

अर्थज्ञानसाधनानि तु निगमनिरुक्तव्याकरणादीनि सन्त्येव । परं तेष्वकुतबहुळपरि-
श्रमाणां माष्यं विज्ञार्थज्ञानं न मुळभमिति मत्वा वैदिकमार्गेप्रवर्तैकस्य वीरशुक्कमुपते-
र्मन्त्रिणा पाखवाचार्येण चत्वारोऽपि वेदा व्याख्याताः । तत्र यजुर्वेदव्याख्याने तस्य
पञ्चमोऽध्यायोऽयं रुद्राऽध्यायोऽपि व्याख्यातः । तत्र च केवळं रुद्राध्यायार्थविवि-
त्सूनां समग्रस्य तस्य ग्रन्थस्य संपादनं बहुवित्तव्ययायाससाध्यमिति मत्वा सुळमत्वे-
नास्य प्रचारो मुग्यादिति विचार्योऽऽनन्दाश्रमाधिपतिना प्रथमं सायणमाष्यसहित एवायं
रुद्राध्यायो मुद्रितुमारव्धः । एतस्मिन्नेव समये वाईक्षेत्रनिवासिभिः गङ्गाधरअनन्त
नित्सुरे इत्यभिधैयैन्मित्रैर्भट्टमास्करकृतभाष्यसहितमेकं पुस्तकं मत्सविधे प्रेषितम् ।
एतददृष्टपूर्वमतीव सभीचीनं मट्टपास्करं माष्यं विद्रुपां सुळभं भवलिति विचार्य तद्-
प्यत्र संगृहीतम् । तदेवं माष्यद्वयसंवळितोऽयं रुद्राध्यायः सर्वेषां पठनपाठनार्थज्ञाना-
दिभिः कर्माङ्कत्वं ज्ञानहेतुत्वं च साधयलिति ।

अत्र प्रसक्ततो मावत्राचार्यस्य किञ्चिद्रूत्तं वर्ण्यते । तत्र दक्षिणस्यां दिशि कर्णाटक-

CC.0- Jangamwadi Math Collection. Digitized by eGangotri
( ३ )

देशे तुङ्गभद्रानदीतीरे पम्पानामकं सरः क्षेत्रं च वर्तते । अस्यैव विजयानगरमिति
प्रथा पूर्विस्मिन्काल आसीत् । इयमेव कैश्चिकिञ्चन्शानगरीत्यपयिमधीयते संप्रति
तु गोवळकोण्डा इति प्रसिद्धमेतन्नगरं यत्र च विरूपाक्षारूपया स्वदर्शनेन तत्रत्याञ्च-
नान्पावयति स्म भगवाञ्शंकरः । तत्र वैदिकमार्गप्रवर्तकः स्वप्रतापरिशोषितपरि-
पन्थिसरा वीरबुक्कभूपालो नाम सार्वभौम आसीत् । तस्येन्द्रस्य बृहस्पतिरिव नछस्य
सुमितिरिव रामस्य वसिष्ट इव धर्मसुनस्य धौम्य इव पृथोः सौजामिनिरिव कुल-
गुरुर्मंश्री राजकार्यधुरन्धरोऽयं माघवाचार्य आसीत् । यस्य च विहसितबृहस्पति-
शुक्रचाणक्यादिनीतिविभवस्य शास्त्रे लौकिके व्यवहारे च महती प्रतिष्ठाऽऽसीदत
एव माघवाथेमाघवाचार्यमाघवामात्यइत्यादीन्यन्वर्थकानि नामान्युपलभ्यन्ते तेषु तेषु
ग्रन्थेषु + । अस्य च—

+ अत्र ताम्रपत्रस्थितमिदमनुसंधेयम्—
तस्मिन्मारतवर्षेदक्षिणदले देशोऽस्ति कर्णाटक-
स्तन्मध्ये सरिदुत्तमा विजयते सा तुङ्गभद्राभिधा ।
यत्तीरे दुरिताटवीहुतवहे देशे विरूपाक्ष इ-
त्यास्ते संततमन्तरेण सुमनःस्तोतास्त्विनीचितनम् ॥ १ ॥
यस्योपकण्ठे विजयाभिधाना सा दुर्नेया राजति राजधानी ।
यस्यां विरूपाक्षकटाक्षदग्धमुज्जीवयन्ति स्मरमुत्पलाक्षयः ॥ २ ॥
वंशे यदोरभिनवांश इवाच्युतस्य श्रीसङ्गमाद्जनितसङ्ककरङ्गभीमः ।
श्रीवृक्कराज इति विक्कृतशञ्चुरेण तामष्यतिष्ठद्रय मध्यमलोकपालः ॥ ३ ॥
तस्याज्ञया माघवमन्त्रिवर्यः प्राशाञ्जयन्नीपुरराज्यमृढम् ।
यन्मन्त्रशक्त्या वपुर्त्सृजन्तोऽप्यरातयः स्वास्थ्यमहो भजन्ते ॥ ४ ॥

आशान्तिविश्रान्तयशाः स मन्त्री दिशो जिगीषुर्महुता बलेन ।
गोवाभिधां कौङ्कणराजधानीमन्येन मन्येऽरुणद्र्णवेन ॥
प्रतिष्ठितांस्तत्र तुरुष्कतस्त्र्घातुत्साद्य दोष्णा भुवनैकवीरः ।

उन्मूछितानामकरोत्प्रतिष्ठां श्रीसत्तनाथादिसुषाभूजां यः ॥ ५ ॥ इत्यादि ।

स्वस्ति श्रीशके त्रयोदशाधिकत्रिशतोत्तरसहस्र ( १३१३ ) गते वर्तमानप्रजाप-
तिसंवत्सरे वैशाखमासे कृष्णपक्षेऽमावास्यायां सौम्यदिने सूर्योपरागपुण्यकाले स्वस्ति-
श्रीमन्महामन्त्रीश्वर उपनिषन्मार्गप्रवर्तकाचार्यः श्रीमन्माघवराजः कुञ्जरविषयवर्तीनं
कुञ्चरनामानं ग्रामं माघवपुरिमिति प्रथितनामानमग्रहारं कृत्वा, इत्यादिविस्तरमया-
दलं पश्चित्तेन ।

( ४ )

' श्रीमती जननी यस्य सुकीर्तिपॉयणः पिता ।
सायणः सोमनाथश्च मनोवुद्धी सहोद्रौ ॥ १ ॥
यस्य वौधायनं सूत्रं शाखा यस्य च याजुषी ।
भारद्वाजं यस्य गोत्रं सवेज्ञः स हि माधवः ' ॥ २ ॥

इति पराशरस्मृतिव्याख्याप्रकमे स्वयमेव वतेनैवोक्तवाच्छ्रीमती जननी मायणः पिता
सायणः सोमनाथश्चेमौ द्वौ भ्रातरौ वौधायनं सूत्रं याजुषी शाखा भारद्वाजं गोत्रं
चाऽऽसीदिति ज्ञायते । कुत्रचित् ' सायणो मोमनाथश्च ' इत्यपि पाठान्तरं दृश्यते
तस्मात्सायणसोमनाथसंज्ञकौ वा सायणभोगनाथसंज्ञकौ भ्रातराविति निश्चेतुं न शक्यते
तथा च वेदार्थप्रकाशो माघवाचार्यकृतः सायणाचार्यकृतो वेत्यपि न ज्ञायते । केचिदा-
धुनिकाः पण्डिताः —माधवाचार्यस्तावद्वानकार्येनिमग्न एव आसीत् । तस्य च भ्राता
सायणाचार्यस्तेनैव वेदार्थप्रकाशादीन्महतोऽनेकान्ग्रन्थानिर्माय माघवाचार्येनाम्ना प्रसिद्धि
प्राप्ता यथा राज्ञो देवगिरीश्वरस्यामात्यो हेमाद्रिः स्वाश्रितपण्डितवरचोपदेवकृतै-
र्बहुभिग्रन्थैः स्वनाम्ना प्रस्यापितैर्मन्थकुदिति प्रसिद्धि गतस्तथैवायं माधवाचार्यैः
सायणकृतग्रन्थैः प्रसिद्धि गत इति वदन्ति । वस्तुतस्तु नात्र किमपि प्रमाणमुपलभा-
महे यद्राश्रित्यास्यानुमानस्य प्रमाणकोटौ प्रवेशः स्यात् । माघवाचार्यकृतत्वेन प्रसि-
द्धेषु विभिन्नेषु ग्रन्थेषु कुत्रचित् ' सायणमाघवीये ' ' कुत्रचिन्माघवामात्याविरविराचित
कुत्रचित् ' सायणाचार्यविराचिते माघवीये ' इत्यादुपलभ्यते । अतो वेदार्थप्रकाशा-
दिग्रन्थकर्तो सायणो वा माघवो वेति महान्संशयः । सोमनाथकृता ग्रन्था न कुत्राप्यु-
पलभ्यन्ते । भट्ट सोमेश्वर इत्येको महान्पण्डित आसीत् । तत्कृता बहवो मीमां-
साग्रन्था उपलभ्यन्ते स एव सोमनाथो वाऽऽन्यो वा कश्चनेत्यपि न निश्चेतुं शक्यमत
आस्तां गहनावगाहेन ।

माघवाचार्यजन्मसमयः शके १३०० तमे वर्षे आसीदिति केचन वदन्ति । उप-
लव्धताम्रपत्रलेखे १३१३ प्रजापतिनाम्नि संवत्सरे वैशाखकृष्णपञ्चेऽमायां सूर्यग्रहणे
वैदिकमार्गप्रवर्तकश्रीमन्माघवाचार्येण कुच्चरसंज्ञकस्य ग्रामस्य स्वनाम्ना माघव पुरमिति
नाम निष्याय चतुर्विंशतिसंख्येयभ्यो विद्वद्भ्यो ब्राह्मणेभ्य उदकपूर्वकं तद्दत्तामिति
ज्ञायते । तस्माच्च १३१३ तमे वर्षे एतद्दानं दत्तमिति गृहीतं चेत्त्रयोदश शतकस्य
चतुर्थे चरणे तस्य जन्माऽऽसीदित्यनुमित भवति ।

माघवाचार्येण पूर्वेऽस्मिन्वयसि प्रथमं वीरबुक्कभूपालस्यानन्तरं किंचित्काळपर्यन्तं
तत्सूनोश्च सुग्रहीतनामधेयस्य हरिहराह्वयस्य मन्त्रित्वं कृतिमिति ज्ञायते । यतोऽय-
र्वेणवेदार्थप्रकाशतमातौ हरिहरनामोच्छेखस्तेनैव कृतः । अनन्तरमैहिकसुखानिरपेक्षेण

( ९ )

माधवाचार्येणोत्तरस्मिन्वयस्यैहिकसाम्राज्यधुरं त्यक्त्वाऽऽमुमिकसाम्राज्यधूर्ग्रहणायैव
चतुर्थाश्रमः स्वीकृतः । तत्र विद्यारण्यतीर्थे इति प्रथिताऽस्याभिघा । चतुर्थाश्रमेऽप्यनेन
श्रीमद्भगवतः शंकराचार्यस्य शारदापीठे शृङ्गेवरपुरे तत्पट्टाधिकारः स्वीकृत इति
ज्ञायते । तस्मिंश्च पट्टे शंकराचार्योत्पद्धिंशातितमोऽयं विद्यारण्यभारती आसीदिति ज्ञाय-
ते । चतुर्थाश्रमेऽस्य गुरवः शंकरानन्दस्त्रामिन आसन् । यतस्तत्कृतपञ्चदश्यां "नमः
श्रीशंकरानन्दगुरुपादाम्बुजन्मने" इति शंकरानन्दस्य गुरुत्वेनाभिधानं स्पष्टम् । चतु-
र्थाश्रमग्रहणानन्तरं द्वैताद्वैतविषये वहुभिमतान्तरास्थितैः पण्डितैरस्य विवादोऽभूदिति ।
विशिष्टाद्वैतमतवादिनाऽक्षोभ्यमुनिसंज्ञकेन केनचिन्महापण्डितेन काञ्चीनगरे वहुदिनप-
र्यन्तं विवाद आसीदिति श्रूयते । तत्र च जयापजयविषय उभयमतास्थायिनां महान्क-
लहः । केचन विशिष्टाद्वैतवादिन एवं वदन्ति—

' विद्यारण्यमहारण्यमक्षोभ्यमुनिरच्छिनत् '
इति । अन्येऽद्वैतवादिनः—

' अक्षोभ्यं क्षोभयामास विद्यारण्यो महामतिः '
इत्यादि यथाभिलषितं जल्पन्ति । तदास्तां स्वमताग्रहगृहीतानां जल्पनाविवेचनेन ।
श्रीमन्माघवाचार्यकृता ग्रन्थास्त्वेते—

१ ऋग्वेदभाष्यमू २ यजुर्वेदभाष्यमू ३ सामवेदभाष्यमू ४ अथर्वणवे-
दभाष्यमू ५ चतुर्णां वेदानामैतरेयताण्ड्यादिब्राह्मणभाष्याणि ६ परा-
शरस्मृतिभाष्यमू ७ जैमिनीयन्यायमालाविस्तरः ८ सर्वेदर्शनसंग्रहः ९
अनुभूतिमकाशः १० दशोपनिषद्दीपिका ११ ब्रह्मगीता १२ पञ्चदशी
१३जीवन्मुक्तिविवेकः १४ अपरोक्षानुभूतिटीका १५ माघवरुत्तिः ।
इत्यादयोऽन्ये च वहवस्तत्कृता ग्रन्था उपलभ्यन्ते । स्कन्दपुराणान्तर्गतसूतसंहिता-
व्याख्याकर्ताऽपिमाघव इति प्रसिद्धः । केचन सायणमाघव एव स इति वदन्ति ।
अस्मत्संगृहीतसूतसंहिताटीकापुस्तके—

प्रणमामि परं ब्रह्म यतो व्यावृत्तवृत्तयः ।
अविचारासहं वस्तु विषयी कुर्वते धियः ॥ १ ॥
श्रीमत्काशीविलासाख्यक्रियाशक्तीशसेविना ।
श्रीमत्त्र्यम्वकपादाब्जसेवानिष्णातचेतसा ॥ २ ॥
वेदशास्त्रमतिष्ठात्रा श्रीमन्माधवमच्छिणा ।
तात्पर्यदीपिका सृतसंहिताया विधीयते ॥ ३ ॥
क्रियते तत्प्रकरणतात्पर्यमथनपूर्वकं विचरदमू ।
विषमपदवाक्यविवरणं मन्दधियामनुग्रहाय भक्त्या च ॥ ४ ॥

( ९ )

इति वर्तते । तस्मात्काशीनिवासिना अ्यन्चकपादाञ्चसेविना केनचिन्माघवसंज्ञकेन
विदुषेयं सृतसंहिताटीका निर्मितेत्यनुमीयते । किंच माघवाचार्येकृतग्रन्थस्य—

' वागीशाद्याः सुमनसः सर्वार्थानामुपक्रमे ।
यं नत्वा कृतकृत्याः स्युस्तं नमामि गजाननम् ' ॥ १ ॥

इत्ययं श्लोको राजशास्त्रनमुद्रिकेव प्रायस्तत्कृतेषु ग्रन्थेषु सर्वत्रोपलभ्यते सृतसंहि-
ताटीकायां स न कुत्रापि दृश्यते । तथैव माघवनिदानकर्तोऽप्यन्य एव । शतप्रश्न-
कश्पञ्चतिकाकृन्माघवः कश्चनातीवाचीन इति प्रतिभाति ।

अथ द्वितीयं माण्यं मटमास्करकृतम् । अथ कोऽयं मटमास्करः कुत्रत्यश्चेत्यादि-
विवक्षायामस्येतिवृत्तः किमपि न ज्ञायते । अनेन सर्वस्यापि यजुर्वेदस्य माण्यं कृतम् ।
श्रीमच्छंकरभगवत्पादपूज्यस्थितिसमये मट्टमास्करसंज्ञकः कश्चन शास्त्र आसीदिति माघ-
वाचार्ये कृतशंकरविजयसंज्ञकाद्ग्रन्थाज्ज्ञायते । तस्मिन्नेव समयेऽन्योऽपि मटमास्करो
ज्योतिःशास्त्रप्रवर्तक आसीदिति श्रीमदानन्दगिरिकृतशंकरविजयादवगम्यते । एतयो-
रन्यतम एवायमितिस्वीकारे न किंचित्प्रमाणमुपलभामहे । किंच शंकराचार्यसमकाली-
नस्यास्यातिप्राचीनत्व एतत्कृतयजुर्वेदमाण्यादीनां श्रीमन्माघवाचार्येकृतवेदार्थप्रकाशा-
दिग्रन्थेषु न कुत्राप्युछेखः । मट्टगुरुसंज्ञको मीमांसकधुरीणो महान्पण्डित आसीदिति
वैदार्थप्रकाश उक्तम् । स च मट्टकुमारिल एवेति विदुषां मतम् । तथाऽपि यजुर्वेद-
माण्यकृञ्टमास्करस्तु कुत्र कदा वा जात इति निर्णेतुमशक्ता वयम् । तथाऽपि
मट्टमास्करोऽयं माघवाचार्यात् प्राचीन इति तु निश्चितमेवेति विद्रो विदांकुर्वन्तु ।
तदेवं सायणाचार्येमट्टमास्करकृतमाण्यद्वयसंवलितोऽयं रुद्राध्यायो विदुषां मोदमाव-
हन्सर्वैतो विजयतामित्यभ्यर्थ्यते—

तत्रभवतां सेवाभिलाषी
वामनशास्त्रिशर्मा ।

आदर्शपुस्तकोल्लेखपत्रिका ।

ऋद्राध्यायपदानामादर्शपुस्तकं ' क ' इतिसंज्ञितं पुण्यपत्तननिवासिभिः
' केशव मल्हार गुरुजी ' इत्येतैर्दत्तम् ।
सायणाचार्यकृतरुद्राध्यायभाष्यस्याऽऽदर्शपुस्तकानि पञ्चैवोपलब्धानि ।
तानि यथा—
   क. ख. संज्ञिते—वे० शा० संपचगृहाघरशाखिदाताराभिषैर्दत्ते ।
      ग. संज्ञितं—वे० शा० संपत्रवासुदेवशाखिभिर्दत्तम् ।
      घ. संज्ञितं—पुण्यपत्तननिवासिभिः आपटे इत्युपनामधारिमहादेविच-
            मणाजि इत्येतैर्दत्तम् । तच्च शालिवाहनशके १७३९
            ईश्वरनामसंवत्सरे आषाढशुद्धनवम्यां लिखितम् ।
      ङ. संज्ञितं—जयपूरनिवासिभिर्नारायणभट्टपर्षणीकर इत्येतैः प्रेषितम् ।
            भट्टभास्करकृतरुद्राध्यायभाष्यस्याऽऽदर्शपुस्तकद्वयमेवोप-
            लब्धम् । तद्यथा—
   क. संज्ञितं—वटोद्रनिवासिभिः   श्रीमद्भाप्पासाहेबमैराळ   इत्येतैः
         प्रेषितम् ।
   ख. संज्ञितं—वाईसेत्रनिवासिभिर्नित्मुरे इत्येतैः प्रेषितम् ।
   आपटे इत्येषां पुस्तकं मुक्त्वाऽन्येषां पुस्तकानां लेखनसंग्रहादिकालो
नैव दृष्टः ।

वामनशाखिशर्मा ।

तृतीयावृत्तिमुद्रणप्रस्तावः ।

अथास्य रुद्रभाष्यस्य तृतीयमुद्रणाग्रतिविषये किंचिद्विद्वद्बृन्देभ्यो विज्ञाप्यते।
अस्य ( द्वितीय ) मुद्रणकाले मुद्रणकर्तृणां शोधनकर्तृणां वाऽनवधानत्वाद्यत्र
यत्र स्खलितमभूत्तत्र तत्र निखिलं संशोध्य यथा वाचकजनमनोमोदावहं
स्यात्तथा तत्र तत्र स्थलेषु संस्कृतेयं तृतीयावृत्तिर्मुद्रिता । अस्यामप्यनवधान-
तया स्खलितपवलोकनपथमागतं चेत्किं क्षम्यतामित्येवमभ्यर्थ्यते ।
                                             हरि नारायण आपटे .

॥ श्रीः ॥
ॐ नमः श्रीगणेशाय
अथ रुद्राध्यायः

सायणाचार्य-भट्टभास्करभाष्यसहितः ।

सायणाचार्यकृत उपोद्धातः ।

यस्य निःश्वसितं वेदा यो वेदेभ्योऽखिलं जगत् ।
निर्ममे तमहं वन्दे विद्यातीर्थमहेश्वरम् ॥ १ ॥
इष्टकाचितयः सर्वाश्चतुर्थे हि समापिताः ।
रुद्राध्याये पञ्चमे तु चित्यमौ होम उच्यते ॥ २ ॥
कर्मप्रकरणे पाठात्कर्माङ्क्रत्वमपीष्यते ।
ज्ञानहेतुत्वमप्यस्य तथोपनिषदीरितम् ॥ ३ ॥
किंजप्येनामृतत्वं नो ब्रूहीत्युक्तो मुनिर्जगौ ।
शतरुद्रीयकेणेति जाबाला आमनन्ति हि ॥ ४ ॥
स्मृत्यागमपुराणेषु रुद्राध्यायप्रशंसनम् ।
बहुस्ति तद्विस्तरेण रुद्रकल्पेऽभिधास्यते ॥ ५ ॥
इह कर्माङ्गता याहगवणिता ब्राह्मणेन ताम् ।
वोढुं शब्दार्थमात्रस्य विवृतिः क्रियते स्फुटा ॥ ६ ॥

कल्पः — 'शतरुद्रीयं जुहोति जतिलयवाग्वा वा गवीधुकयवाग्वा वा जति-
लैगवीधुकसक्तुभिः कुशयवसपिंपाऽजाक्षीरेण मृगीक्षीरेण वाऽकैपर्णेनोदरु-
तिषृच्चुत्तरस्य पक्षस उत्तरापरस्यां सक्त्यां विकण्यां स्वयमातृणायामनुपरि-
चारं वा । नमस्ते रुद्र मन्यव इत्येतानुवाकांश्चैधं विभज्यापि वा प्रथमाऽनुप-
क्रम्य नमस्तक्षम्य इति जातुदग्ने धारयमाणो रथकारेभ्यश्च वऽइत्युपंक्रम्य नमः
स्वायुधायेति नाभिदग्ने शेषेण प्रागवेताँनभ्य आस्यदग्ने हुत्वा सहस्राणि सह-
स्रा इति दर्शावतानांहुत्वाऽन्वारोहान् जुहोति नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथि-

१ क. घ. 'स्यसबोंप। २ ख. ड. तत् । ३ ङ. क्तुभिवां कु°। ४ ख. रिकरं। ५ घ.
°षताने । ६ ख. शाऽनुवाकान्हु°।

२ सायणाचार्यें–मद्भास्करभाष्यसहितः— [न०म०अ०]

ञ्यामिति जानुदग्ने धारयमाणो नमो रुद्रेभ्यो येऽन्तरिक्ष इति नाभिदग्ने नमो
रुद्रेभ्यो ये दिवीत्यास्यदग्ने हुत्वैतानेव यजमानं वाचयित्वा एतानेव विपरीतान्
मत्यरोहानहुत्वा संचरे पशुनामर्कपर्णमुदस्यति यं द्विष्याचस्य संचरे ' इति ।।
मद्भास्करकृत उपोद्धातः

श्रीगणेशाय नमः ।। ॐ अतः परमग्निकाण्डमेवाग्न्यार्पेयं तत्र चरमायामिष्ट-
कायां '' शतरुद्रीयं जुहोति नमस्ते रुद्र मन्यव इति । रुद्रो वा एष यदग्निः
स एतहिं जातः '' इत्यादि ब्राह्मणम् । ''रुद्रो वा एष यदग्निस्तस्यैते तनुवा''
इत्यादि च ' अजाक्षीरेण जुहोति ग्राम्याणां पशूनां पयसा जुहुयात् ।' इत्या-
दिब्राह्मणम् । ' अर्कपर्णेन जुहोति ' इति । ' अङ्गिरसः सुवर्गे लोकं यन्तः '
इत्यादि ब्राह्मणम् । तत्रायं प्रयोगक्रमः—उदङ्मुखस्तिष्ठन्मुखदग्ने
धारयन् ' नमस्ते रुद्र मन्यवे ' इति प्रतिपद्यान्तमेतमनुवाकं तत्रिगद्य द्वितीय-
तृतीयौ चतुर्थस्य यत्राभिजानाति ' नमः क्षत्रुभ्यः ' इति ततः स्वाहा करोति
ततः मागाद्रत्य नाभिदग्ने धारयन् ' संग्रहीतृभ्यश्च वः ' इति प्रतिपद्यावशिष्टम-
नुवाकस्य निगद्य पञ्चमं षष्ठं सप्तमस्य च यत्राभिजानाति ' नमो वष्यार्य च'
इति ततः स्वाहा करोति । ततो दक्षिणाद्रत्य जानुदग्ने धारयन् ' नमो वष्यार्य
च ' इति प्रतिपद्यावशिष्टमनुवाकस्य निगद्याष्टमं नवमं दशमं चैकादशस्य च
यत्राभिजानाति । 'य एतावन्तश्व' इति ततः स्वाहा करोति । '' त्रेधा विभक्तं
जुहोति'' इत्यादि ब्राह्मणम् । 'इयत्यग्रे जुहोति' इत्यादि च । तत्रैकाशीतिभागाः
षण्णवतिमन्त्राः । ततः मत्यङ्गाद्रत्य गुल्फदग्ने धारन् 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथि-
ञ्याम्' इति हुत्वैतद्यजमानं वाचयति ''तिस्र उत्तरा आहुतीर्जुहोति '' इत्यादि-
ब्राह्मणम् ''एता वै देवताः सुवर्गां या उत्तमाः'' इत्यादि च । नाभिदग्ने धारयन्
' नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये दिवि' इति हुत्वा यजमानं वाचयति ।। अस्य रुद्राध्याय-
स्य ऋषिर्भँगवानेव, अग्निः काण्डऋषिः, छन्दो महाविराट्, देवताऽपि च शंभुः ।
प्रतिमञ्चग्रूष्यादयो ञ्यारूयावसरे वक्ष्यन्ते । एष यज्ञसंयुक्तः कल्पः । अथवा
यज्ञसंयुक्ताः प्रयोगाः स्मृतिपुराणादिषु प्रसिद्धा मोक्षधर्मपापक्षयविचा-
रोग्यायुष्यादिफला बह्वो विद्यन्ते तेषु च काश्चिद्नृपः । प्रतिमञ्च च प्रयो-
गविशेषाः पुरस्ताद्रक्ष्यन्ते । तत्र जावाळोपनिषदि—'अथ हैनं ब्रह्मचारिण
ऊचुः । किञ्जप्येनैवामृतत्वमश्नुत इति ब्रूहीति स होवाच याज्ञवल्कयः शत-
रुद्रियेण' इति 'एतानि ह वा अमृतस्य नामधेयानि भवन्ति एतैर्हे वा अमृतो
भवति' इति । कैवल्योपनिषदि 'यः शतरुद्रियमधीते सोऽग्निपूतो भवति

[न०प्र०अ०] रुद्राध्यायः । ३

स्वर्णस्तेयात्पूतो भवित सुरापानात्पूतो भवित ब्रह्महत्यात्पूतो भवित कृत्याकृ-
त्यात्पूतो भवित तस्मादिवमुक्तमाश्रितो भवित अत्याश्रमी सर्वदा सकृद्वा जपेत् ।
         ''अनेन ज्ञानमामोति संसाराणर्वनाशनम् ।
          तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं कैवल्यं फलमश्नुते ॥
          इति कैवल्यं फलमश्नुते '' इति ।
आह शातातपः–''स्तेयं कृत्वा गुरुदारांश्च गत्वा मद्यं पीत्वा ब्रह्महत्यां च कृत्वा ।
          भस्मच्छन्नो भस्मशय्याशयानो रुद्राध्यायी मुच्यते सर्वेपापैः'' । इति ।
आह याज्ञवल्कयः—

         ''सुरापः स्वर्णहारी च रुद्रजापी जले स्थितः ।
          सहस्रशीर्षजापी च मुच्यते सर्वकिल्विषैः ॥
          शुक्रियारण्यकजपो गायत्र्याञ्च विशेषतः ।
          सर्वपापहरा ह्येते रुद्रैकादशिनी तथा ॥
          वेदमेकगुणं जप्त्वा तदग्नैव विशुध्यति ।
          रुद्रैकादशिनीं जप्त्वा तदग्नैव विशुध्यति "इति ॥
आह शङ्कः:—"सुवर्णस्तेनो रुद्राध्यायी मुच्यते" इति ।
आह्तुरत्नपञ्जिरसौ —"एकादशगुणान्वाऽपि रुद्रानाहृत्य धर्मिवत् ।
          महापापैरपि स्पृष्टो मुच्यते नात्र संशयः" इति ।
वायुमोक्तपुराणे—"यश्च रुद्राञ्जपोनित्यं ध्यायमानो महेश्वरम् ।

          यश्च सागरपर्यन्तां सशैलवनकाननाम् ॥
          सर्वात्मगुणोपेतां सुवृक्षजलशोभिताम् ।
          दद्यात्काञ्चनसंयुक्तां भूमिं चौषधिसंयुताम् ॥
          तस्मादप्यधिकं तस्य सकृद्रुद्रजपाद्भवेत् ।
          ममभावं समुत्छड्य यस्तु रुद्राञ्जपेत्सदा ।
          स तेनैव च देहेन रुद्रः संजायते ध्रुवम् ॥
          जपेनानेन विप्रेन्द्रा नृपाश्चान्ये द्विजातयः ।
          हित्वैवं सकलं पापं गताः शिवपुरं पुरा ।
          अहं च परमेशस्य सामीप्यं लब्धवांस्तथा'' ॥ इति ॥
तथा—"चमकं नमकं चैव पौरुषसूक्तं तथैव च ।
          नित्यं त्रयं प्रयुञ्जानो ब्रह्मलोके महीयते'' ॥
तथा—"चमकं नमकं होतृनुपुरुषसूक्तं जपेत्सदा ।
          भविश्रेष्ठं महादेवं गृहं गृहपतिर्यथा" ॥

४ सायणाचार्ये–भट्टभास्करभाष्यसहितः— [न०प्र०अ०]

तथा—''भस्मदिग्धशरीरस्तु भस्मशायी जितेन्द्रियः ।
सततं रुद्रजाप्योऽसौ परां मुक्तिमवाप्स्यति ॥
रोगवान्पापवांश्चैव रुद्रं जप्त्वा जितेन्द्रियः ।
रोगात्पापाद्विनिर्मुक्तो ह्यतुलं सुखमश्नुते '' ॥

आह शङ्खः—"रहसि कृतानां महापातकानामपि शतरुद्रीयं प्रायश्चित्तम्''
इति । आहाऽऽपस्तम्बः—"यस्य रुद्रः प्रजां पशून्वाऽभिमन्येतोऽदङ्ङ् परेत्य
रुद्राज्ञपश्वरोदित्ययज्ञसंयुक्तः कल्पः '' इति । ईशानयज्ञे च "अत्र रुद्राज्ञपे-
त्मयमोत्तमो वा'' इति। अयं च ब्रह्मविद्यारूपत्वादुपनिषन्मन्त्रराशिः मन्त्रः ।
अतएव रहस्यप्रकरण आम्नायते । ब्राह्मणविनियोगानुरोधेन तत्र व्याचक्ष्महे।
एकशतमध्वर्युशाखास्तासु सर्वासु चेयं रुद्रोपनिषदाम्नायते । शतं रुद्रा देवता
अस्येति शतरुद्रीयमुच्यते शतरुद्राद्धत्येति च्छप्रत्ययः । अनया च रुद्रोप-
निषदा ब्रह्मविद्या प्रतिपाद्यते।ब्रह्मणश्च त्रीणि रूपाणि सन्ति । एकं कार्यरूपं
सर्वोपादानतया सर्वात्मकम् । इतरत्पुरुषार्थ्यं सृष्टिस्थितिसंहारनिमित्तम् ।
द्वयमप्याविद्यकमन्यतपरं रूपं निर्गुणं निरञ्जनं सत्यज्ञानानन्दलक्षणम् । तदेतद्-
विषणाऽप्युक्तशू "विश्वं भूतं भुवनं चित्रं बहुधा जातं जायमानं च यत् । सर्वो
ह्येष रुद्रः'' इति। "यो देवानां प्रथमं पुरस्ताद्विश्वा धियो रुद्रो महर्षिः'' इति ।
उक्तं च —

"संसारी चिदचिद्भयोऽन्यः कश्चित्कश्चिदनाविलम् ।
जयत्यनादिनिधनो महादेवो महापिवे'' इति ॥
किंच—"पूर्वपष्टकमभिष्ठाय विभर्तीदं चराचरम् ।
आत्मत्रयमधिष्ठाय सृष्टच्यादि प्रकरोति सः '' ॥

तत्र योऽसौ संहारकर्ता तं सर्वेदा देवोऽधितिष्ठति । कार्यकाले त्वन्या सा
च देवस्य घोरा तनूः । अन्या शिवा । यथोक्तम्—' रुद्रो वा एष यदग्निस्त-
स्येते तनुवौ घोराऽन्या शिवाऽन्या' इति। "यच्छतरुद्रीयं जुहोति यैवास्य घोरा
तनूस्तां तेन शमयति '' इत्यादि च । शक्तिश्च सर्वदा विचित्रा संहारकारिणी
तस्य संहारकर्तृपश्रुतयो देवस्य घोरास्तनवस्तासु भयशङ्का । तस्मात्प्रथमेना-
नुवाकेन तादृशं देवं कुद्धं मसाद्य तस्योपसंहारकरणानि च नम इत्यादिभिर्न-
मस्कृत्य मसादयति । यथोक्तम्—"यैवास्य घोरा तनूस्तां तेन शमयति। यद्-
सोर्षीरां जुहोति यैवास्य शिवा तनूस्तां तेन प्रीणातीति ततस्तामुपसंहार्ये
द्वितीयमभूतिभिरष्टाभिरनुनाकैर्देवस्य वैश्वरूप्येण स्तुतिं नमस्कारं च करोति ।
एवं च देवः सुतरां मसीदति ततो दशमैकादशाभ्यामभयं याचते"। अयं मः

rudradhyaya.english:
Om, Salutations to the Supreme Soul.

According to the saying "They praise with Rik verses, sacrifice with Yajus, and sing with Saman," this fifth chapter of the Yajurveda, which is dedicated to sacrifice, is called the 'Rudradhyaya'. In the fourth chapter, all the brick-pilings (for the altar) were described. In this fifth chapter, named Rudradhyaya, the oblation (homa) in the piled fire is described. Since it is recited in the Karma-kanda (ritual section), it is a part of the ritual, as the Shruti states:

"This Agni is indeed Rudra; he is born when he is fully piled up. Just as a newborn calf desires the breast, so he (Agni-Rudra) at this time desires his share. If one does not offer an oblation to him, he might harm the Adhvaryu and the sacrificer. One offers the Shatarudriya; by that share, one pacifies him. The Adhvaryu and the sacrificer do not suffer harm."

The Smriti also says:
"He who daily employs the triad—Chamakam, Namakam, and the Purusha Suktam—is glorified in the world of Brahma."

Yajnavalkya also states:
"One who drinks wine or steals gold, if he stands in water and chants the Rudra (Namakam) and the 'Sahashra-shirsha' (Purusha Suktam), he is freed from all sins."

Thus, its status as a part of the ritual is well-known. In the Jabala Upanishad, its role as a cause of knowledge is also mentioned. When asked by students, "By chanting what does one attain immortality?", Yajnavalkya replied, "By the Shatarudriya." "These are indeed the names of Immortality; by these one becomes immortal." In the Kaivalya Upanishad, it is said: "He who studies the Shatarudriya becomes purified by fire," and so on.

Smritis also state:
"One who constantly chants the Rudra will attain supreme liberation," and many others.

Thus, because of its role in both ritual and knowledge, this Rudradhyaya must be revered by both those seeking worldly results and those seeking liberation. Its recitation and teaching are prevalent everywhere, especially among the people of the South. Although it yields desired results just through recitation, as the Veda says:

"One, though seeing, has not seen Speech; another, though hearing, does not hear her. To another, she reveals her beauty, like a well-dressed, loving wife to her husband."

Because of the praise for understanding the meaning of the Vedas in such verses:

"What is learned but not understood, and is merely repeated by rote, never blazes up, like dry wood in a place without fire."

( 2 )

Because of the hearing of censure for those who do not know the meaning, as stated in verses like:

"He who has studied the Veda but does not know its meaning is indeed a pillar (sthanu) carrying a burden. But he who knows the meaning attains all auspiciousness and reaches heaven, his sins being shaken off by knowledge."

By such sources, one who does not know the meaning is censured as being like a pillar, while one who knows the meaning is said to attain all well-being. Therefore, knowing the meaning is absolutely necessary. "Sthanu" (pillar) is the example. A dry tree stump with its branches cut off is called a "sthanu". Some believe that ritual status is achieved through correct recitation alone, even without knowing the meaning. They argue that the injunction "One's own Veda should be studied" is fulfilled just by grasping the syllables of the Veda. Knowledge of the meaning of the former section (Karma-kanda) is implied by the performance of rituals. Since ritual performance happens through recitation alone, that view is generally accepted there. However, since verses like "One who does not know the meaning and has a weak voice... these are the lowest of reciters" describe the one who doesn't know the meaning as the lowest of reciters, even recitation cannot be perfect without knowledge of the meaning. Although ritual performance is achieved by the mere chanting of mantras, the Chandogas (followers of the Samaveda) declare that a ritual performed with knowledge brings greater results: "Therefore, both perform it—he who knows this thus and he who does not know. But knowledge and ignorance are different. What one performs with knowledge, faith, and the Upanishad (meditation), that alone becomes more potent (viryavattara)." "Study" (Adhyayana) means grasping the syllables preceded by (or along with) the knowledge of their meaning. If that is true, the injunction to study is fulfilled only by grasping the syllables with knowledge of their meaning, not by mere grasping of syllables. The status as a cause of knowledge cannot exist without knowing the meaning. Among modern scholars, there is no disagreement at all regarding the necessity of knowing the meaning for everyone. Thus, it is established that even for its ritual role, grasping the meaning of this Rudradhyaya is necessary.

The means for knowing the meaning include the Nigamas, Nirukta, Vyakarana (Grammar), etc. However, considering that for those who have not put in much effort in those subjects, it is not easy to understand the meaning without a commentary (Bhashya), the minister of King Virabukka, Madhavacharya (also known as Vidyaranya), who was a promoter of the Vedic path, explained all four Vedas. In his explanation of the Yajurveda, this fifth chapter, the Rudradhyaya, was also explained. Thinking that for those who only wish to know the meaning of the Rudradhyaya, acquiring that entire massive work would involve great expense and effort, and considering that its spread should be easy, the head of Anandashrama first began printing this Rudradhyaya along with Sayana's commentary (Sayana-Bhashya). At this very time, a friend named Gangadhar Anant Nitsure, a resident of Wai, sent me a book containing the commentary by Bhatta Bhaskara. Considering that this previously unseen and very excellent commentary by Bhatta Bhaskara should be easily available to scholars, it has also been included here. Thus, this Rudradhyaya, combined with both commentaries, will serve everyone's needs for recitation, teaching, and understanding the meaning, thus fulfilling its role in both ritual and knowledge.

Here, incidentally, some account of Madhavacharya is described. In the southern direction, in the Karnataka...

( 3 )

In that region, on the banks of the Tungabhadra river, there is a lake and a sacred field named Pampa. In former times, its name was famous as Vijayanagara. Some call this same city Vidyanagari. At present, it is famous as Golconda (though the text likely refers to the ruins near Hampi/Vijayanagara, as it mentions Virupaksha). There, Lord Shankara, in the form of Virupaksha, purifies the people of that place by his sight. There lived a sovereign king named Virabukka, a promoter of the Vedic path, who had dried up the ocean of his enemies by his prowess. Just as Brihaspati was to Indra, Sumati to Nala, Vashistha to Rama, Dhaumya to the son of Dharma (Yudhisthira), and Saujamini to Prithu, so was this Madhavacharya his family preceptor (Kulaguru), great minister, and the bearer of the burden of state affairs. He, whose wealth of policy surpassed that of Brihaspati, Shukra, and Chanakya, held great prestige in the scriptures and in worldly affairs. Therefore, meaningful names like Madhavabhatta, Madhavacharya, and Madhavamatyah are found for him in various works+. Regarding him—

+ This (inscription) from a copper plate should be considered here:
In this land of Bharatavarsha, in the southern part, there is the Karnataka country; in its midst flows the excellent river named Tungabhadra. On its bank resides Virupaksha, the fire to the forest of sins, constantly worshipped by the assembly of gods... (1)
Near it shines the capital city named Vijaya (Vijayanagara), hard to be conquered by enemies. In that city, lotus-eyed women bring back to life Cupid (Smara), who was burnt by the glance of Virupaksha... (2)
In the Yadu lineage, like a new part of Achyuta (Vishnu), was born King Bukka from Sri Sangama, a hero like Bhima in battle. By the speed of his commands, he ruled the middle world... (3)
By his command, the excellent minister Madhava ruled the prosperous kingdom of Chandragiri. By the power of his counsel, even enemies who give up their bodies (die), alas, attain peace! (4)

That minister, whose fame rested at the ends of the quarters, desiring to conquer the directions with a great army, surrounded the capital of Konkan named Goa with another ocean, as I think... (5)
Having uprooted the Turks (Taurushkas) established there, that sole hero of the world, by his arm, established the uprooted images of deities like Saptanatha... (6) etc.

Hail! In the Shaka year one thousand three hundred and thirteen (1313) [1391 AD], in the current Prajapati year, in the month of Vaishakha, in the dark fortnight, on the day of the new moon (Amavasya), a Wednesday, at the holy time of a solar eclipse, the illustrious great minister and teacher who promoted the path of the Upanishads, Sri Madhavaraja, having made the village named Kunjara in the Kunjara district into an Agrahara (land grant to Brahmins) with the famous name Madhavapuri... enough of this expansion.

( 4 )

"Whose mother is Shrimati, and whose father is of good fame, Mayana; Sayana and Somanatha are his brothers, like mind and intellect. (1)
Whose ritual manual is the Baudhayana Sutra, whose branch is the Yajushi (Yajurveda), and whose lineage (Gotra) is Bharadwaja—that all-knowing one is indeed Madhava." (2)

As he himself stated in the beginning of his commentary on the Parashara Smriti, it is known that his mother was Shrimati, father was Mayana, and Sayana and Somanatha were his two brothers; his sutra was Baudhayana, branch was Yajushi, and Gotra was Bharadwaja. In some places, the reading "Sayana and Bhoganatha" is also seen. Therefore, it cannot be determined with certainty whether the two brothers were named Sayana and Somanatha or Sayana and Bhoganatha. Similarly, it is not known whether the "Vedartha-prakasha" (the commentary on the Vedas) was authored by Madhavacharya or Sayanacharya. Some modern scholars say that Madhavacharya was entirely absorbed in state affairs, and his brother Sayanacharya, having composed great works like the Vedartha-prakasha, achieved fame under Madhavacharya's name. Just as Hemadri, the minister of the King of Devagiri, is famous as an author through many works actually composed by his protégé, the great scholar Vopadeva, but published in his own name, so also they say this Madhavacharya became famous through works composed by Sayana. In reality, we find no evidence on which this inference could enter the category of proof. In the various works famous as being authored by Madhavacharya, we find titles like "Sayana-Madhaviya" in some places, "Composed by the Minister Madhava" in others, and "In the Madhaviya composed by Sayanacharya" in yet others. Therefore, there is a great doubt as to whether the author of works like the Vedartha-prakasha is Sayana or Madhava. Works composed by Somanatha are not found anywhere. There was a great scholar named Bhatta Someshwara; many Mimamsa works composed by him are found. Whether he is the same Somanatha or someone else cannot be determined. So, let it be; enough of this deep immersion.

Some say the time of Madhavacharya's birth was around the Shaka year 1300. From an available copper plate inscription, it is known that in the Shaka year 1313, in the year named Prajapati, on the day of the solar eclipse in the dark fortnight of Vaishakha, the illustrious Madhavacharya, a promoter of the Vedic path, having renamed the village named Kunjara as Madhavapuri after himself, gave it to twenty-four learned Brahmins with a libation of water. If we take this gift as being given in the year 1313, it is inferred that his birth was in the fourth quarter of the thirteenth century (Shaka).

It is known that in his early life, Madhavacharya first served as a minister for King Virabukka, and later for some time for his son, the one of auspicious name, Harihara. This is because he himself mentions the name of Harihara in the conclusion of the Atharvaveda-prakasha. Later, indifferent to worldly pleasures...

( 5 )

In his later years, Madhavacharya gave up the burden of worldly empire to take up the burden of the spiritual empire and accepted the fourth stage of life (Sannyasa). In this stage, his famous name was Vidyaranya Tirtha. In the fourth stage, it is known that he accepted the headship of the Sharada Peetham at Sringeri, established by the illustrious Bhagavan Shankaracharya. It is known that he was the twenty-fourth in succession of Sannyasis from Shankaracharya on that seat, named Vidyaranya Bharati. His guru in the fourth stage was Shankarananda Swamin. This is clear from the mention of Shankarananda as his guru in his work Panchadashi: "Salutations to the lotus-born feet of the Guru, Sri Shankarananda." After accepting Sannyasa, he had many debates on the subjects of Dvaita and Advaita with scholars belonging to other schools. It is heard that there was a long debate in the city of Kanchi with a great scholar named Akshobhya Muni, a proponent of the Vishishtadvaita school. There was great conflict regarding victory and defeat among the followers of both schools. Some Vishishtadvaitins say:

"Akshobhya Muni cut down the great forest (Aranya) of Vidyaranya."

Others, the Advaitins, chatter as they please:

"The great-minded Vidyaranya agitated (kshobhayamaasa) the unagitated (Akshobhya)."

Let it be; enough of analyzing the babble of those gripped by partiality for their own views. The works composed by the illustrious Madhavacharya are these:

1. Rigveda Bhashya, 2. Yajurveda Bhashya, 3. Samaveda Bhashya, 4. Atharvaveda Bhashya, 5. Commentaries on the Brahmanas of the four Vedas like Aitareya, Tandya, etc., 6. Parashara Smriti Bhashya, 7. Jaiminiya Nyayamala Vistara, 8. Sarvadarshana Sangraha, 9. Anubhuti Prakasha, 10. Dashopanishad Deepika, 11. Brahma Gita, 12. Panchadashi, 13. Jivanmukti Viveka, 14. Aparokshanubhuti Tika, 15. Madhava Vritti.

Many other such works composed by him are found. The commentator on the Suta Samhita within the Skanda Purana is also famous as Madhava. Some say he is the same Sayana-Madhava. In the manuscript of the Suta Samhita commentary collected by us:

"I bow to the Supreme Brahman, from whom functions have turned away; whom thoughts make their object, though it is a reality that cannot bear (is beyond) reasoning. (1)
By the illustrious minister Madhava, a devotee of the Lord of Power known as Kashi-vilasa, whose mind is expert in serving the lotus feet of the illustrious Tryambaka (Shiva)... (2)
The establisher of the Vedic scriptures, the illustrious minister Madhava, composes the 'Tatparya-deepika' (Lamp of Meaning) on the Suta Samhita. (3)
This work is made after churning the essential meaning of that section, explaining difficult words and sentences for the grace of the slow-witted and out of devotion." (4)
...

( 6 )

...thus it is. From this, it is inferred that this Suta Samhita commentary was composed by some scholar named Madhava who was a resident of Kashi and a devotee of the feet of Tryambaka. Moreover, in the works of Madhavacharya:

"I bow to Gajanana, whom the lords of speech and other gods worship at the beginning of all undertakings to achieve success." (1)

This verse, like a royal seal, is found almost everywhere in the works composed by him. In the Suta Samhita commentary, it is not seen anywhere. Similarly, the author of the Madhava-nidana (a medical work) is also someone else. The Madhava who composed the Shata-prashna-kalpa-latika appears to be someone quite recent.

Now for the second commentary, by Bhatta Bhaskara. Regarding the question of who this Bhatta Bhaskara was and where he was from, nothing is known of his history. He composed a commentary on the entire Yajurveda. From the work named Shankara-vijaya composed by Madhavacharya, it is known that during the time of the venerable Bhagavan Shankaracharya, there was a scholar named Bhatta Bhaskara. From the Shankara-vijaya composed by the illustrious Anandagiri, it is understood that at that same time, there was another Bhatta Bhaskara who was a promoter of the science of Jyotisha (astronomy/astrology). We find no evidence to accept that this (commentator) is one of those two. Furthermore, if he were so ancient as to be contemporary with Shankaracharya, there is no mention of his Yajurveda commentary anywhere in the Vedartha-prakasha and other works composed by the illustrious Madhavacharya. In the Vedartha-prakasha, it is mentioned that there was a great scholar, a leader among Mimamsakas, named Bhattaguru. Scholars believe that he is indeed Bhatta Kumarila. Nevertheless, we are unable to determine where or when Bhatta Bhaskara, the author of the Yajurveda commentary, was born. Still, let the learned know for certain that this Bhatta Bhaskara is earlier than Madhavacharya. Thus, it is requested that this Rudradhyaya, combined with the two commentaries by Sayanacharya and Bhatta Bhaskara, may bring joy to the learned and be victorious everywhere.

Desirous of serving your honors,
Vamanashastri Sharma.

( 7 )

List of Original Manuscripts (Adarsha-pustaka).

The manuscript of the words of the Rudradhyaya, designated as 'Ka', was given by Keshav Malhar Guruji, a resident of Pune.
Five manuscripts of Sayanacharya's commentary on the Rudradhyaya were obtained.
They are as follows:
   'Ka' and 'Kha'—Given by Vedashastra Sampanna Gangadhar Shastri Datar.
   'Ga'—Given by Vedashastra Sampanna Vasudev Shastri.
   'Gha'—Given by Mahadev Chimnaji Apte, a resident of Pune. It was written in the Shalivahana Shaka year 1739, in the year named Ishwara, on the ninth day of the bright fortnight of Ashadha.
   'Nga'—Sent by Narayan Bhatta Parshvanikar, a resident of Jaipur.
Only two manuscripts of Bhatta Bhaskara's commentary on the Rudradhyaya were obtained.
They are as follows:
   'Ka'—Sent by the illustrious Bappasaheb Mairal, a resident of Vadodara.
   'Kha'—Sent by the Nitsure family, residents of the Wai region.
Except for the book from the Apte family, the time of writing or collection of the other books was not seen.

Vamanashastri Sharma.

Preface for the Third Edition.

Now, a small submission is made to the assembly of scholars regarding the third printing of this Rudra-bhashya. At the time of the (second) printing, wherever errors occurred due to the inattentiveness of the printers or proofreaders, those have been entirely corrected in this third edition, printed after being refined in various places so as to be pleasing to the minds of the readers. If any errors are still seen due to inattentiveness, we request to be forgiven.

Hari Narayan Apte.

|| Sri ||
Om, Salutations to Shri Ganesha.
Now begins the Rudradhyaya,
along with the commentaries of Sayanacharya and Bhatta Bhaskara.

Introduction (Upodghata) by Sayanacharya.

"I bow to Maheshwara (the Great Lord), who is the Lord of the Ford of Knowledge (Vidyatirtha), whose breath is the Vedas and who created the entire world from the Vedas. (1)
All the brick-pilings (for the altar) were completed in the fourth (chapter). In the fifth, named the Rudradhyaya, the oblation (homa) in the piled fire is described. (2)
Since it is recited in the ritual section (Karma-prakarana), its status as a part of the ritual is also established. Its role as a cause of knowledge is likewise stated in the Upanishads. (3)
When asked, 'Tell us, by what chanting do we attain immortality?', the Sage (Yajnavalkya) replied, 'By the Shatarudriya.' Indeed, the Jabala Upanishad followers state this. (4)
The praise of the Rudradhyaya in the Smritis, Agamas, and Puranas is extensive; that will be described in detail in the Rudra-kalpa. (5)
To support its ritual application as described in the Brahmana section, a clear explanation of the words and meanings is provided here." (6)

Kalpa (Ritual Manual):
"One offers the Shatarudriya with wild sesame gruel (jatila-yavagu) or coix gruel (gavidhuka-yavagu), or with wild sesame and coix flour, or with kusha grass, barley, and ghee, or with goat's milk or deer's milk, using a leaf of the Arka plant. [The ritual instructions continue, describing the position near the fire altar—the northern-western corner, the self-formed brick (svayamatrunna), etc.] One divides the anuvakas starting from 'Namaste rudra manyava...' and offers them. Or, starting from the first and offering up to 'Namah takshabhyah...' holding (the oblation) knee-high in the fire; and starting from 'Rathakarebhyashcha vah...' up to 'Namah svayudhaya...' holding it navel-high; and with the remainder, moving forward... Having offered after the mantra 'Sahasrani sahasra...', one offers the Anvaroha (concluding) oblations with 'Namo rudrebhyo ye prithivyam'..."

2  Sayanacharya—Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya-Sahita— [Na. Ma. Am.]

...holding at knee-height (with 'Namo rudrebhyo ye prithivyam'); holding at navel-height (with 'Namo rudrebhyo ye antarikshe'); and holding at face-height (with 'Namo rudrebhyo ye divi'), and after offering, one makes the sacrificer (Yajamana) recite these. Having offered these in reverse order as 'Pratyarohas' (descents), one casts the Arka leaf on the path (samchara) of animals, or where the one whom he hates walks."

Introduction (Upodghata) by Bhatta Bhaskara.

Salutations to Shri Ganesha. Om. Henceforth begins the Agni-kanda (section on Fire), which is dedicated to Agni. There, at the last brick-piling, "one offers the Shatarudriya with 'Namaste rudra manyava...'. This Agni is indeed Rudra; he is born then," says the Brahmana text. "This Agni is indeed Rudra; these are his bodies," etc., and "He offers with goat's milk; one should offer with the milk of domestic animals," etc., says the Brahmana. "He offers with an Arka leaf." "The Angirases, going to the heavenly world," etc., says the Brahmana. There, this is the procedure of application (prayoga-krama): Standing facing North, holding (the oblation) at face-height, starting with "Namaste rudra manyave..." and reciting that anuvaka to the end, then the second and third, and up to where he recognizes "Namah kshatrubhyah" in the fourth, then he utters "Svaha" (and offers). Then moving East, holding at navel-height, starting from "Samgrahitribhyashcha vah..." and reciting the remainder of that anuvaka, then the fifth and sixth, and up to where he recognizes "Namo vashyarya cha" in the seventh, then he utters "Svaha". Then moving South, holding at knee-height, starting from "Namo vashyarya cha" and reciting the remainder of that anuvaka, then the eighth, ninth, and tenth, and up to where he recognizes "Ye etavantashcha" in the eleventh, then he utters "Svaha". "He offers divided into three," says the Brahmana. "He offers at such a height," etc. In that, there are eighty-one parts and ninety-six mantras. Then moving West, holding at ankle-height, having offered with "Namo rudrebhyo ye prithivyam," he makes the sacrificer recite it. "He offers the three higher oblations," says the Brahmana. "These deities are indeed heavenly, those which are the highest," etc. Holding at face-height (or eye-height), having offered with "Namo rudrebhyo ye divi," he makes the sacrificer recite it.

For this Rudradhyaya:
The Rishi (Seer) is the Lord (Bhagavan) Himself.
The Kanda-Rishi (Section-Seer) is Agni.
The Metre (Chhandas) is Mahavirat.
The Deity (Devata) is Shambhu (Shiva).
The Rishis etc. for each individual mantra will be mentioned during the explanation. This is the ritual procedure connected with the sacrifice (Yajna). Or, there are many applications (prayogas) famous in the Smritis and Puranas with results such as the path to liberation, destruction of sins, health, longevity, etc.; some of those will be mentioned. The specific applications for each mantra will be described later. There, in the Jabala Upanishad: "Then the students said to him: 'Tell us, by what chanting alone does one attain immortality?' That Yajnavalkya said: 'By the Shatarudriya.'" "These are indeed the names of Immortality; by these one becomes immortal." In the Kaivalya Upanishad: "He who studies the Shatarudriya becomes purified by fire..."

[Na. Pra. Am.] Rudradhyaya. 3

"...becomes purified from the theft of gold; becomes purified from the drinking of liquor; becomes purified from the killing of a Brahmin; becomes purified from performing or not performing actions (as required). Therefore, one who has taken refuge in this becomes as described. One who is beyond the stages of life (Atyashrami) should chant it always or even once.
          'By this one attains knowledge that destroys the ocean of worldly existence.
          Therefore, knowing this thus, one enjoys the fruit of liberation (Kaivalya).'
          Thus, 'one enjoys the fruit of liberation.'"

Shakatatapa says: "Having committed theft, and having gone to the Guru's wife, having drunk liquor, and having committed the killing of a Brahmin—one who is covered in ashes, lying on a bed of ashes, and reciting the Rudradhyaya is freed from all sins."

Yajnavalkya says:
         "A drinker of liquor or a stealer of gold, standing in water and chanting the Rudra, or chanting the 'Sahasra-shirsha' (Purusha Suktam), is freed from all sins.
          The chanting of the Shukriya Aranyaka and especially the Gayatri—these are indeed removers of all sins, and so is the Rudra-ekadashini (reciting Rudram eleven times).
          By chanting the Veda once, one is purified even then; by chanting the Rudra-ekadashini, one is purified even then."

Shankha says: "A stealer of gold is freed by reciting the Rudradhyaya."

Atri (or Ratna-panjara) says: "Having brought together the Rudras eleven times like a righteous man, even if touched by great sins, one is freed; there is no doubt in this."

In the Vayu-prokta Purana (Vayu Purana):
"He who chants the Rudras daily, meditating on Maheshwara...
(compared to) one who would give as a gift the earth up to the ocean, with its mountains and forests, endowed with all natural qualities, beautiful with excellent trees and water, along with gold and medicinal herbs—greater than even that is the merit from a single chanting of the Rudra.
He who, having abandoned the sense of 'I' and 'mine', always chants the Rudras, surely becomes Rudra even in that very body.
By this chanting, O lords of Brahmins, kings and other twice-born ones, having left all sin, went to the city of Shiva in the past. I too have obtained proximity to the Supreme Lord in this way."

Also: "He who daily employs the triad—Chamakam, Namakam, and the Purusha Suktam—is glorified in the world of Brahma."

Also: "One should always chant Chamakam, Namakam, and the Purusha Suktam... to the Great Lord (Mahadeva), the best of beings, just as a householder stays in his house."

4  Sayanacharya—Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya-Sahita— [Na. Pra. Am.]

Also: "With body smeared with ashes, sleeping on ashes, having conquered the senses, constantly chanting the Rudra—he will attain supreme liberation.
The diseased and the sinful, having chanted the Rudra and conquered their senses, are freed from disease and sin and enjoy incomparable happiness."

Shankha says: "The Shatarudriya is the atonement even for great sins committed in secret."

Apastamba says: "If Rudra should afflict one's progeny or cattle, one should go North and chant the Rudras—this is a ritual procedure not connected with the (main) sacrifice."

In the Ishana-yajna: "Here one chants the Rudras once, middle-wise, or the best way (multiple times)."

And since this is of the nature of the Knowledge of Brahman (Brahma-vidya), this collection of mantras is an Upanishad. Therefore, it is recited in the Rahasya (Secret/Upanishadic) section. We explain it here in accordance with its ritual application in the Brahmanas. This Rudra-Upanishad is recited in all the one hundred and one branches of the Adhvaryu (Yajurveda). It is called "Shatarudriya" because its deity is a hundred Rudras. Through this Rudra-Upanishad, Brahma-vidya is taught.

Brahman has three forms:
One is the effect-form (karya-rupa), which, being the material cause of everything, is the self of all.
The other is for the sake of human goals (purusharthya), as the cause of creation, preservation, and destruction.
Both of these are within the realm of ignorance (avidya). The other, the supreme form (param-rupa), is beyond these, without qualities (nirguna), untainted (niranjana), and characterized by Truth, Knowledge, and Bliss.

This has been said by the seers: "The universe, all beings, the wonderful world, whatever was born in many ways and whatever is being born—all this is indeed Rudra." "Rudra, the great seer, who first from the beginning (perceived) all the gods and their thoughts."

And it is said:
"Victorious is Mahadeva, the Great Lord, who is other than the transmigrating self and the insentient, who is pure, and without beginning or end."
Furthermore: "Presiding over the former group of six, he bears this moving and unmoving world. Presiding over the triad of selves, he performs creation etc."

There, the one who is the Destroyer is always presided over by the God. At the time of action, there is that other fierce (ghora) body of the God. The other is auspicious (shiva). As it is said: "This Agni is indeed Rudra; he has two bodies, one fierce and one auspicious." "In that one offers the Shatarudriya, he pacifies his fierce body by that," and so on.

The power of destruction is always manifold. Those positions of the God as the Destroyer are his fierce bodies; in them, there is fear and doubt. Therefore, by the first anuvaka, one pacifies such an angry God and offers salutations with "Namah" to his means of destruction (weapons etc.) to please him. As it is said: "By that, he pacifies his fierce body. In that he offers the 'Shiva-tanu' (auspicious body) mantra, he pleases his auspicious body." Then, having withdrawn that (fierce form), he offers praise and salutations to the God's universal form (vaishvarupya) through the next eight anuvakas (second to ninth). Thus, the God is greatly pleased. Then, through the tenth and eleventh anuvakas, one asks for fearlessness. This is...

ॐ
