translations.sayana:
96 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Da. A.]

(Bhatta Bhaskara continued). || 5 ||

[Purashcharana for this Mantra]: Having fasted for three days, one should chant the mantra 5,000 times to complete the Purashcharana. This should be used for the protection of one's own people. Sacrifice 10,000 offerings of barley and sesame smeared with ghee. Sacrifice 10,000 Udumbara sticks. Sacrifice 10,000 offerings of ghee. Every year, sacrifice 10,000 offerings of ghee. By doing this, there will be peace for one's women, children, and elderly. For other pacification rituals, chant 10,000 times. Especially, a king should have 10,000 offerings of ghee made every month for brilliance; by this, the king's inner apartments etc. also flourish. Meditation:

"Meditate on the deity with a crescent moon on His crown, with a body like the morning sun, in the form of Nandishvara, surrounded by the Lords of Ganas."

He states the sixth Rik:

Do not harm our children or grandchildren, nor our life, nor our cows, nor our horses. O Rudra, do not kill our heroes in your anger. We serve you with salutations, bringing offerings. || 6 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: O Rudra, do not harm ('ma ririshah') our children (tokam) or grandchildren (tanaye). Do not harm our life (ayushi). Do not harm our cows (goshu). Do not harm our horses (ashveshu). Do not kill our heroes (virah/servants) being angry ('bhamitah'). We, bringing offerings (havishmantah), serve you with salutations ('namasa'). || 6 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
138                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

[The verb] "rīriṣaḥ" (do not harm) applies throughout. "Do not harm our ukṣantam" — the son capable of sustaining the family, possessing the power to nourish — "and our ukṣitam" — the son yet in the womb — "and do not harm our pitaram" (father); also do not harm "naḥ priyāḥ tanavaḥ" (our dear bodies). The particle "ā" placed at the beginning serves as a negative (niṣedha, prohibitive) particle, functioning as an alternative to "mā." The plural "naḥ" (our/us) is used with reference to one's own people. Thus the meaning is: do not harm the great ones and the lesser ones among our kinsfolk.

The seer (ṛṣi) of this mantra is Bhṛgu. The deity (devatā) is Rudra in the form of Nandiśvara (the Lord Nandi). The meter (chandas) is Jagatī. Meditation verse: "One should meditate upon Nandiśvara — the god wearing a crescent moon as his crown, whose form blazes like the newly risen sun, and who is upheld by the lord of the gaṇas (attendant hosts)." This mantra is primarily oriented toward peace (śānti). Mantra-siddhi (perfection in the mantra) is attained by reciting it five thousand times, preceded by a three-day fast.

    mā nas toke tanaye mā na āyuṣi mā
    no goṣu mā no aśveṣu rīriṣaḥ | vīrān
    mā no rudra bhāmito 'vadhīr viṣmanto
    namasā vidhema te ||

    Do not harm our offspring (toke), our son (tanaye),
    do not harm our life span, our cattle, or our horses.
    O Rudra, do not in your anger slay our heroes.
    We, bearing oblations (havis), would worship you with obeisance.

Now the sixth mantra — beginning "mā nas toke" (do not harm our offspring). O Rudra, you who have become angry (bhāmitaḥ) on account of transgressions — do not harm (mā rīriṣaḥ) in the domain of our offspring (toke), whether progeny in general or specifically our son (tanaye, the male child). Likewise, do not harm in our lifespan (āyuṣi), our cattle (goṣu), or our horses (aśveṣu).

चनसमर्थं पुत्रम्, उत नः उक्षितं गभेस्थं पुत्रम्, नः
पितरं [अन्वर्थिं] माव भीः; नः प्रियाः तनुवः शरीराणि

ब्दः । स्वकीयजनापेक्षया न इति बहुवचनम् । तथा च सम

अस्य मन्त्रस्य भृगुकृत्षिः । नन्दीश्वरात्मको रुद्रो देवता ।

मन्त्रः । उपवासत्रयपूर्वेकं पश्चसहस्रजपान्मन्त्रसिद्धिः ॥

    मा नस्तोके तनये मा न आयुषि मा
नो गोषु मा नो अश्वेषु रीरिषः । वीरा-

translations.sayana:
98 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Da. A.]

(Purashcharana for Mantra 6 continued): Perform the pacification ritual (Shanti) in months with favorable stars (Saumya Nakshatra) for things like horses etc. Sacrifice 10,000 Ashvattha and Apamarga sticks smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. One whose life is ebbing away gets his life restored. In gambling or disputes, one who chants this mantra is never defeated. Fast for three nights and chant continuously. Then, eating only Charu, sacrifice 10,000 Bilva fruits smeared with honey; one obtains 1,000 gold pieces. Sacrifice 10,000 lilies (kumuda) smeared with curd, honey, and ghee; one obtains a daughter. Living on Charu for a year, sacrifice 8,000 or 108 offerings of cow-ghee daily in kindled fire; one is freed from all great sins. Enchant sandalwood powder with this mantra and wear it on the forehead; everyone becomes submissive. Sacrifice 8,000 Apamarga sticks on every birthday; one conquers untimely death. One desiring rain should worship Surya with hibiscus flowers, wearing red clothes and anointing himself with red paste on a Sunday; the Lord Sun will send great rain. One traveling a long distance should take ten pieces of sugar, enchant them 8,000 times, and throw them in the ten directions; he will be safe throughout the journey. One desiring no obstacles in work should chant the mantra 10,000 times before starting; he will complete the work without effort. One desiring a son should sacrifice 100,000 offerings of ghee; he obtains a son who continues the lineage. To defeat anyone, make their form out of sand, stand on its head, and chant 10,000 times; one will certainly be victorious. Everything else is also possible through this. The power of the mantra bears fruit for those directly perfected by the Lord's grace. Meditation:

"One should always meditate on Rudra, whose body is divided eleven-fold, brilliant like pure crystal, a treasure of light, holding a trident, and having the moon on His head."

He states the seventh Rik:

Let your fierce form which kills cattle and men be far away. Let your auspicious/happiness-giving form be with us. O God, protect us, speak for us, and grant us peace in both worlds. || 7 ||

sanskrit.samhita: स्तु॒हि श्रु॒त-ङ्ग॑र्त॒सद॑-ययुवा॑न-म्मृ॒गन्न भी॒ममु॑पह॒त्नुमु॒ग्रम् । मृ॒डा ज॑रि॒त्रे रु॑द्र॒ स्तवा॑नो अ॒न्यन्ते॑ अ॒स्मन्निव॑पन्तु॒ सेनाः᳚ ॥

translations.sayana:
100 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Da. A.]

[Purashcharana for this Mantra]: Having fasted for three nights, one should chant the mantra continuously for three nights. This completes the Purashcharana. This is only for those desiring long life, not others. As the Shruti says: "Man is called a hundred-year-being." Thus, for those whose life is being taken away by deeds causing decay in this or other births, this ritual is for increasing their life. Daily sacrifice 8,000 or 108 offerings of ghee in the fire. Sacrifice 8,000 Durva grasses smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. Daily chant 8,000 times on one's birth star. Specifically, on the birthday, sacrifice 10,000 each of Durva, Apamarga, Shami, and sesame seeds smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. At night, chant 5,000 times and perform circumambulations. A king should sacrifice 10,000 each of Durva, Apamarga, etc., on his birthday; he will be long-lived. For those unable, sacrifice in a flowing stream; for those extremely weak, only chanting. Sacrifice 10,000 offerings of pure rice smeared with honey and ghee; his life increases. Worship the Lord with 10,000 Arka flowers; his prosperity increases. Staying in a temple with a west-facing door, eating only Charu, sleeping on the ground, being celibate, and eating only unsalted food—chant 1,000 times daily near the Lord; he will live for a hundred years in seven births. For the weak, living on alms is recommended. Meditation:

"Meditate on the Lord dancing the Tandava of bliss in front of the Goddess (Devi), focused on the sacrificial fire, brilliant like molten gold."

He states the eighth Rik:

Praise the famous, the one who resides in the heart, the youthful, fierce like a lion, the destroyer, the fierce one. O Rudra, being praised, make us happy in this aging body. Let your armies strike someone other than us. || 8 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: O my speech, praise the famous, well-known Rudra. What kind? 'Gartasadam', always residing in the lotus of the heart. As the Smriti says: "The Lord dwells in the heart of all beings." 'Yuvanam', ever-youthful. 'Upahatnum ugram', having a fierce form to destroy the entire world at the time of dissolution. An example: 'Bhimam mrigam na', like a terrifying lion. Just as a lion is fierce to tear apart an elephant, so is He. O Rudra, being praised by our words, make this aging body—which decays day by day—happy ('mrida'). Let your armies ('senah') strike ('nivapantu') our enemies, not us. || 8 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    141
---

— blazing like refined gold." This mantra grants long life and prosperity. Mantra-siddhi is attained by continuous recitation for three days and nights, preceded by a three-night fast.

    stuhi śrutaṃ gartasadaṃ yuvānaṃ mṛgaṇṇa bhīma-
    mupahṛtyugram | mṛḍā jaritre rudra stavāno
    'nyan te asmin niṣapantu senāḥ ||

    Praise the renowned one, who dwells in the heart-cave, ever youthful,
    who, like a lion, is fearsome and terrible to those who transgress.
    O Rudra, being praised, bring joy (mṛḍa) to one who is aged.
    May your armies fall upon another, not upon us.

Now the eighth mantra — beginning "stuhi" (praise). Here the poet addresses his own self: O my soul (ātman), praise (stuhi) the Lord. Or alternatively: O speech (vacas), praise! The reason why the Lord deserves praise is stated — "śrutam" (the renowned). He is renowned throughout all the worlds as the Supreme Lord (Parameśvara). One need not fear that such a great one would be unapproachable by my praise, because "gartasadam" — he who ever abides in the cave (garta) of the heart — "ī śvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe 'rjuna tiṣṭhati" (The Lord abides in the heart-region of all beings, O Arjuna) — as stated in the Smṛti (sacred scripture). Not only is he to be praised for his supremacy alone, but also for his eternally youthful, beautiful form — thus says "yuvānam" (ever youthful). Having thus established the manner of praise, the poet indicates the undesirable consequence of not praising him — "upahṛtyugram" (the terrible one who withdraws/destroys). Those who disregard the duties of varṇa (social order) and āśrama (stages of life) — the devotees who are known by their defining marks — he withdraws (upasaṃhartum) them. The simile is given — "mṛgaṇṇa bhīmam" (terrible like a lion). The particle "na" (like) indicates comparison: terrible and fearsome like a lion that has risen to tear apart an elephant. And lest one think that even such a great one as the Lord might himself be overpowered by some stronger being — thus eliminating the reason for praise — the text says "ugram" (the Terrible). He is one whom none can overpower.

translations.sayana:
102 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Da. A.]

He states the ninth Rik:

May the weapon of Rudra avoid us. May the ill-will of the fierce and sinful one avoid us. O Bountiful one, unbend your firm bows from us. O Showerer of desires, make our children and grandchildren happy. || 9 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: 'Hetih' is the weapon by which one strikes. May Rudra's weapon avoid ('pari vrinaktu') us; let it keep away from us. Meaning, let it never strike us. May the ill-will ('durmatih')—the fierce intellect—of Rudra, who is fierce ('tveshasya') and desires sin/suffering ('aghayoh') for those to be punished, also avoid us. Whatever firm bows You have to destroy opponents—O Bountiful one ('maghavan')—unbend them ('avatanushva') from us (the sacrificers). O 'Midhuh' (Showerer of desires), make our son ('tokaya') and his son ('tanayaya') happy. || 9 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The Chhandas of "Pari no rudrasya" is Trishtubh. Rishi and Devata are Bhagavan Himself. Here too, because of the fierce form, the first and second halves are indirect and direct respectively.

"Pari no rudrasya": 'Hetih' is a weapon. May Rudra's weapon avoid ('pari vrinaktu') us and our sons and grandsons. (Grammatical notes). Furthermore, may the ill-will ('durmatih')—the intellect of punishment—of the Lord who is 'tveshasya' (glowing with anger) and 'aghayoh' (desiring suffering for sinful men) also avoid us. The prayer is: "May we not be sinful men who are to be punished." (Grammatical notes). Furthermore, 'sthira': firm bows. (Grammatical notes). Unbend ('avatanushva') the firm bow, meaning relax the string. Or unbend Your many bows. We pray for safety. 'Maghavadbhyah': For the sake of those having wealth (in the form of Havis). Unbend the bow for them; do not harm them. Who harms a sacrificer? The idea is: "Just as we, the offerers of Havis, may be fearless worshippers of Yours, for that purpose, unbend the bow." Others say: "The firm intellect is referred to as 'durmatih'; that firm/unwavering ill-will should not be given to us." Relax it for the sake of the 'maghavat' (sacrificers). 'Midhuh': O Showerer of desires. Make our son ('tokaya') and his son ('tanayaya') happy. (Grammatical notes). || 9 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    143
---

— who bestows fearlessness and boons upon the frightened, who is praised by hosts of gods." Mantra-siddhi (perfection in the mantra) is attained by three nights of continuous recitation preceded by a one-night fast. This mantra is primarily for the destruction of enemies (śatru-nāśana).

    pari ṇo rudrasya hetir vṛṇaktu pari tveṣasya
    durmatir aghāyoḥ | ava sthirā maghavan
    jastanuṣya mṛḍus tokātha tanayāya mṛḍaya ||

    May the weapon (heti) of Rudra pass us by and spare us;
    may the wicked intent of the blazing one who desires evil (aghāyoḥ) circle away from us.
    O bountiful one (maghavan), lay down your firm (sthirā) missile.
    Be gentle toward our offspring (toka) and bring joy to our son (tanaya).

Now the ninth mantra — beginning "pari ṇo rudrasya" (may the weapon of Rudra pass us by). May the weapon (hetiḥ, the missile) of Rudra pass us by (parivṛṇaktu) and leave us alone — may it never enter us. But since it seems impossible to deflect the weapon when the destroyer has the intent to destroy, the prayer continues — "pari tveṣasya" (may the wicked intent also pass by). Of aghāyoḥ — meaning: one who desires harm (agha, sin) as the instrument of his anger; of the blazing (tveṣasya) Rudra who is inflamed by wrath — may even his "durmatir" (wicked intent, the intent to slay) also pass around us. The sequence is: sin arising from non-performance of enjoined acts and from performance of prohibited acts; from that, the anger of the Supreme Lord; from that, the intent to destroy; from that, the infliction of suffering by the Supreme Lord's instruments — in the form of the means of this-worldly and otherworldly suffering, such as time, place, person, etc.; and finally, the experience of pain. Thus the entire cycle from sin to the suffering of its consequences is what is being prayed against here. But the weapon of the Lord is irresistible and unerring — how then can it be averted? To this he answers — "ava sthirā" (lay down the firm one). O mīḍvas (you who shower upon those who bow to you — this being a vocative that contains the reason within itself) —

translations.sayana:
[Na. Pra. Am.] Rudradhyaya. 21

|| He speaks the thirteenth verse ||

That weapon of yours, O most bountiful one, and that bow which was in your hand;
With that, which is non-injurious, protect us from all sides. (13)

[Word-for-word]: Ya | te | hetih | midhuh-tama | haste | babhuva | te | dhanuh || taya | asman | vishvatah | tvam | ayakshmaya | pari | bhuj || 13 ||

Sayana's Commentary: O most bountiful one (midhustama), greatest shedder of desires—that weapon (heti) of yours in the form of a sword etc., and also that bow which was in your hand; with that weapon and with that kind of bow, which is 'ayakshmaya'—not causing injury or disturbance—you protect (paribhuj) us from all sides (vishvatah), guard us entirely. (13)

Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya: Now 'Ya te hetih' and the following three are in Anushtup. The Rishi and Deity are the Lord Himself. O Midhustama—'midhvan' has been explained; the greatest shedder. That weapon (heti) belonging to you, named the bow, which exists (babhuva) in your hand. 'Heti' is derived from the root 'han' (to kill/strike). With that weapon named the bow, which is 'ayakshmaya'—not a cause of disease or injury to devotees. From all causes of fear (vishvatah), you protect (paribhuj) us entirely. Guard us at all times, in all ways, and from all sins. The root 'bhuj' has a Vedic variation. Or: protect all of us (vishvatah), including sons and grandsons. Some say: 'heti' means violence; that violence performed by you, for the sake of which the bow, the means of violence, stays in your hand—protect us from that, etc., is the same. (13)

|| He speaks the fourteenth verse ||

Salutation be to your weapon which is unstrung and powerful;
And also salutation to your two arms and to your bow. (14)

[Word-for-word]: Namah | te | astu | ayudhaya | anatataya | iti | ana-tataya | dhrishnave || ubhabhyam | uta | te | namah | bahubhyam | iti | bahu-bhyam | tava | dhanvane || 14 ||
...
translations.abhinava_shankara:
Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam                   65

---

    "Let the bow of the matted-haired one (Kapardin) be unstrung, and the quiver too be emptied of arrows. Let the arrows of his become incapable of wounding, and let his quiver be swift [but empty]."

Now, even for the arrows that have been blunted, the prayer is offered for their incapacitation: "Vijyam iti" — may the bow of Kapardin (the Lord with matted hair, who is the Supreme Lord), be vijya — without its bowstring; and may the arrow-holder (bāṇavān — one who bears arrows) also be viśalya — devoid of arrows. Even if the quiver holds shafts, there is the concern about arrows elsewhere, hence the [mantra] says: "Aneśan iti" — may the arrows of this Kapardin be incapable of wounding, having attained destruction in the form of inability to pierce (aneśan — may they fail to reach; nāśa in the optative-like usage of the Vedic [aorist]). Furthermore, may his niṣaṅghiḥ (the quiver) be āśu (swift/ready) — meaning capable only of holding the quiver but not qualified to perform the task of pulling out and discharging the arrows. Here, [the text] "Prabhuśca dhanvana" and so forth constitutes a single mantra for the teacher. The seer (ṛṣi) of this pair of [mantras] is Nārada, the metre is Anuṣṭup, and the deity is Śrī Rudra. [The dhyāna-śloka is:] "One should meditate upon Rudra, blazing like ten million rising suns, his form like a flaming fire, his limbs adorned with fearsome serpents, bearing various weapons." Through this [mantra] enemies are destroyed. Thus [ends] the twelfth [mantra].

    "O most boon-granting one (Mīḍhuṣṭama), the weapon (heti) of yours that was in your hand,

translations.sayana:
[Na. Da. A.] Rudradhyaya | 99

[Purashcharana for these two (Riks 3 and 4)]: Having performed a Kricchra penance, one should chant the mantra 9,000 times to complete the Purashcharana. On a New Moon day, having lit the fire at a crossroads, sacrifice 10,000 offerings of thick curd (dadhi-sara); there will be peace for cattle etc. For the peace of royal mounts, having touched water, chant the mantra 10,000 times and sprinkle the stable with that water; there will be peace for the mounts. Or mix that water with whatever is to be used for the mounts. To pacify animals, sacrifice 10,000 sesame seeds mixed with ghee. For the peace of others, sacrifice 10,000 Durva grasses. Meditation:

"Meditate on Soma (the Moon), sitting on a divine throne, being praised by great sages, with a pleased face, wearing a crescent moon."

He states the fifth Rik:

Do not kill our elders, nor our children, nor our young men, nor the unborn. Do not kill our father, nor our mother. O Rudra, do not harm our dear bodies. || 5 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: O Rudra, do not harm ('ma ririshah') our elderly men (sthavira). And also do not harm our infants (arbhaka). Furthermore, do not harm our young men capable of procreation (ukshantam). And do not harm our unborn/fetal men (ukshitam). Do not kill ('ma vadhih') our father. And also do not kill our mother. Do not harm our dear bodies. || 5 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The Rishi of "Ma no mahantam" is Devarata. Chhandas is Jagati. The deity is Rudra in the form of Nandishvara.

"Ma no mahantam": O Rudra, do not kill our elder, like a grandfather etc., or an elderly son, or a son worthy of grace having qualities of knowledge and age. Do not kill through diseases etc. And do not kill our infant child. Do not kill our young man capable of procreation, who continues the lineage. And do not kill the unborn (ukshitam), who is even more pitiable in the womb. Father and mother are most venerable; do not kill them. And do not harm our dear bodies, because of their very dearness. Do not afflict them with diseases. The plural is used for wife, sons, etc. Do not harm our dear bodies. || 5 ||

sanskrit.krama: स॒हस्रा॑णि सहस्र॒शः । स॒हस्र॒शो ये । ये रु॒द्राः । रु॒द्रा अधि॑ । अधि॑ भू॒म्याम् । भू॒म्या-न्तेषाम् । तेषाग्ं॑ सहस्रयोज॒ने । सहस्रयोज॒ने-ऽव॑ । अव॑ धन्वा॑नि । धन्वा॑नि तन्मसि । तन्म॒सीति॑ तन्मसि ॥

translations.sayana:
[Na. Da. A.] Rudradhyaya | 107

He states the twelfth Rik:

Thousands and thousands of ways are your weapons in your arms. O Bountiful one! O Lord of these! Turn their faces away from us. || 12 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam                   59

---

The Blessed Lord, who is of the nature of Āditya (the Sun), and the Moon — and the other Rudras who have become unified with the Sun — these, stationed in all the directions around this earth in accordance with the Lord's command and in consonance with the accumulated merit and demerit of all beings, in order to grant favour and to restrain — these, positioned in the eastern and other directions, numbered in many thousands (as will be stated in the [later mantra] "sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ") — we appease the heḍa (fury arising from transgression of what is prohibited and from improper conduct) of these Rudras, who are of the form of Āditya, through praise, salutation and other devotional acts. May they be pleased with us — such is the meaning. Thus [ends] the seventh [mantra].

    "That one who moves downward, blue-necked and deep red — the cowherds saw him, and the women who carry water saw him; and all living beings saw him; he, being seen, grants us happiness."

Thus having propitiated [the Lord] through the seventh [mantra], now through the eighth, the fulfilment of one's desired object is prayed for: "Asau yo'vasarpati" — He who is known in the Vedic texts such as "trilochanaṃ nīlakaṇṭham" (the three-eyed, the blue-throated one) — that very one is Asau (this one), who, being "Vilohita" (intensely red), moves downward (avasarpati, i.e., proceeds in motion). For what purpose does he move? That is explained next: "Uta enam" — this Lord Supreme, of the form of Āditya, the cowherds —

commentaries_sanskrit.abhinava_shankara:
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                   ५९

---

एवंभूत आदित्यात्मा भगवान्चन्द्र:, ये च अन्ये रुद्रा आ-
दित्येन वहैकीभूता:, इमामू अभित: अस्या भूमे: परित:,
दिक्षु श्रिता: परमेश्वरनियोगेन जनानां पुण्यपापानुरूपतया-
लुमहे निम्रहं च कर्तुं प्राच्यादिदिक्षु स्थिता:, ते च सहस्रश:
अनेकसहस्रसंख्याका: 'सहस्राणि सहस्रश:' इत्यमे वक्ष्य-
माणा:, एषामू आदित्यरूपाणां रुद्राणाम्, हेड: विहिंसतांक-
रणिनिषिद्धसेवानिमित्तकोपम्, अवेमहे स्तुतिनमस्कारादि-
भि: अपनयाम:, ते अस्माकं प्रसन्ना भवन्त्वर्थ: । इति
सप्तम: ॥

    असौ योऽवसर्पति नीलग्रीवो विलो-
हित: । उतैनं गोपा अदृशन्नदृशन्नुदहा-
र्य: । उतैनं विश्वा भूतानि स दृष्टो मृड्-
यति न: ॥

   एवं सप्तमेन प्रसाध अष्टमेनेष्टसिद्धि प्राथ्येते— असौ
योऽवसर्पति इति। य: नीलग्रीव: 'त्रिलोचनं नीलकण्ठम्'
इत्यादिश्रुतिप्रसिद्ध:, स एव असौ विलोहित: विशेषेण
लोहित: सन्, अवसर्पति प्रवर्तते, किमर्थं प्रवर्तते ? तत्राह
— उतैनमू इति । एनमू आदित्यरूपं परमेश्वरम्, गोपा

sanskrit.pada: अ॒स्मिन् । म॒ह॒ति । अ॒र्ण॒वे । अ॒न्तरि॑क्षे । भ॒वाः । अधि॑ ॥
sanskrit.krama: अ॒स्मिन्-म॑ह॒ति । म॒ह॒त्य॑र्ण॒वे । अर्ण॒वे-ऽन्तरि॑क्षे । अ॒न्तरि॑क्षे भ॒वाः । भ॒वा अधि॑ । अधि॒त्यधि॑ ॥

translations.abhinava_shankara:
Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam                   67

---

[continuing the fourteenth mantra commentary]

and repeating "ubhābhyām" (to both) — "and also salutation to your bow (dhanvane)." Thus [ends] the fourteenth [mantra].

    "Let the weapon launched from your bow protect us from all sides. And that quiver of yours — place it here, far away from us, in the midst of our enemies."

Now, after offering salutation, [the devotee] again prays: "Pari te dhanvana iti" — O Blessed Lord, the weapon (heti) — meaning an arrow — belonging to your bow (dhanvanaḥ), and [thus] understood as having been placed upon the bow — let such a weapon (heti) protect us from all distress (viśvataḥ — from all sides), making us well-guarded. But how, when there is a mass of sins present, can protection from distress [caused] by you come about? That is addressed: "Atho — and also, your quiver (iṣudhi) in which your arrows rest — place that (tam dhehī) in our enemies' quarter (āre — in the multitude of adversaries), in the mass of our sins, and destroy [them there]." Thus [ends] the fifteenth mantra. For the pair of verses beginning "Yā te heti" and so forth, the metre is Anuṣṭup; the seer and deity are the Blessed Lord himself. [The dhyāna-śloka is:] "May one perpetually meditate upon the Father of the worlds, the holder of the Pināka (bow), whose lotus feet are reddened with the pollen of the garlands of flowers worn on the crowns of the hosts of prostrating immortals, seated with his consort." This mantra is primarily [efficacious] for the destruction of enemies.

commentaries_sanskrit.abhinava_shankara:
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                   ६७

उभाभ्यामिति पुनर्नेमीति । तथा तव धन्वने नमोऽस्तु
इति। इति चतुर्दश: ॥

    परि ते धन्वनो हेतिरस्मान्वृणक्तु वि-
श्वत: । अथो य इषुधिस्तवारे अस्मिन्-
धेहि तम् ॥

   अथ नमस्कृत्य पुन: प्राथ्यते— परि ते धन्वन इति ।
हे भगवन् ते धन्वन: धनुष: संबन्धिनी, धनुष्यारोपितेति
भावत्; पतादृशी या हेति: बाणरूपायुधम्, सा हेति:
अस्मान् विश्वत: सर्वेस्मादुपद्र्वात्, परिवृणक्तु परिविजि-
तान् करोतु, रक्षित्वित्यर्थ: । कथं पुन: पापराशौ विद्यमाने
तव उपद्र्वपरिहार: स्यात्? तत्राह— अथो अपि च, य:
तव हेषिधि: यत्र लक्ष्णा हेषवस्तिष्ठन्ति, तमू अस्मत् अस्माकं
संबन्घिनि, आरे अरीणां समूहे, पापराशौ, निधेहि स्थापय,
नाशयेत्यर्थ: । इति पञ्चदशो मन्त्र: । अत्र या ते हेतिर-
त्यादिध्यूचस्य अनुष्टुप्छन्द:, ऋषिर्देवता च भगवानेव। 'प्रण-
मदमरसंघमौलिमाळाकुसुमरजोरुणपाद्पद्मयुगसम् । अनवर-
तमनुस्मरेद्रवान्या सह जगतां पितरं पिनाकपाणिम्' ।
शत्रुनाशप्रघानोऽयं मन्त्र: ॥

sanskrit.krama: नील॑ग्रीवा-श्शिति॒कण्ठाः॑ । शिति॒कण्ठाः॑ श॒र्वाः । श॒र्वा अ॒धः । अ॒धः क्ष॑माच॒राः । क्ष॒मा॒च॒रा इति॑ क्षमा-च॒राः ॥

translations.sayana:
16  Sayanacharya—Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya-Sahita— [Na. Pra. Am.]

...or he who bears (bibharti) the universe through the granting of knowledge etc., is 'babhruh'. 'Sumangalah'—endowed with all auspiciousness because he is the cause of it. In the Bahuvrihi compound, the accent is on the final syllable of the last member. Such is this Aditya (Sun). The meaning is: Rudra who is of the form of such a Sun. And those Rudras of similar nature to Rudra, with similar appearance and glory, created by the God and always his attendants—who are spread in the directions around this earth. They exist in the thousands (sahasrashah), becoming groups of thousands. (Their purpose is) to grant the fruits according to the merits and sins of the worlds. Some say: the Rudras in the form of rays of this Sun-formed Rudra, who exist all around the earth—of this Rudra and these attendants of Rudra, we remove (av-imahe) their 'heda'—anger or disrespect. (We do this) with hymns and salutations—this is the implied remainder. Yaska says 'hedah' means anger. Both are taken here: we remove the disrespect toward us arising from the violation of injunctions and the anger arising from the performance of prohibited acts. The root 'ii' (to go) alone is a verb for 'requesting'. As seen in "We always pray (imahe) for a portion," etc. The 'shap' suffix is dropped in the Veda. Combined with 'ava' (av-imahe), it means to remove. Or, we pray in such a way that it (the anger) becomes diminished (avachinam). (7)

|| He speaks the eighth verse ||

That one who moves along, blue-throated and specially red;
And him the cowherds saw, and the water-carrying women saw;
And him all beings saw—he, being seen, makes us happy. (8)

[Word-for-word]: Asau | yah | ava-sarpati | iti | ava-sarpati | nila-grivah | iti | nila-grivah | vi-lohitah | iti | vi-lohitah || uta | enam | gopah | iti | go-pah | adrishan | adrishan | udaharyah | iti | ud-haryah || uta | enam | vishva | bhutani | sah | drishtah | mridayati | nah || 8 ||

Sayana's Commentary: That Rudra who is blue-throated (nilagriva)—possessing a neck of blue color due to the holding of the Kalakuta poison—he indeed, being 'vilohita' (specially red) as he resides in the solar disc...
...

र्य: । उतैनं विश्वा भूतानि स दृष्टो मृड्-
यति न: ॥

   एवं सप्तमेन प्रसाध अष्टमेनेष्टसिद्धि प्राथ्येते— असौ
योऽवसर्पति इति। य: नीलग्रीव: 'त्रिलोचनं नीलकण्ठम्'
इत्यादिश्रुतिप्रसिद्ध:, स एव असौ विलोहित: विशेषेण
लोहित: सन्, अवसर्पति प्रवर्तते, किमर्थं प्रवर्तते ? तत्राह
— उतैनमू इति । एनमू आदित्यरूपं परमेश्वरम्, गोपा

sanskrit.krama: ये वृ॒क्षेषु॑ । वृ॒क्षेषु॑ स॒स्पिञ्ज॑राः । स॒स्पिञ्ज॑रा॒ नील॑ग्रीवाः । नील॑ग्रीवा विलो॑हिताः । विलो॑हिता॒ इति॑ वि-लोहिताः ॥

translations.sayana:
112 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. E. A.]

[Purashcharana for these four Mantras]: Having performed a Chandrayana penance, chant the mantra 5,000 times to purify the body. This completes the Purashcharana. One should perform black magic rituals (Abhichara) with these mantras. A king should have the Homa performed by Brahmins born in his own country. Sacrifice 100,000 sticks of Shalmali, Sala, Vetra, and Benu, wrapped in a single thread and smeared with blood. Here, having made an image of the enemy out of smooth flour, having invoked life into it, then cutting it in order, sacrifice 8,000 offerings. Then sacrifice 8,000 bundles (kurcha) smeared with blood. By these rituals, enemies perish. Countering black magic with these same mantras: sacrifice 5,000 Durva grasses smeared with curd, honey, and ghee. The defects of black magic affecting the king or the performer will perish. Meditation:

"Meditate on the one with a fierce fanged face, with blazing fire as hair, holding a trident and a lamp, and adorned with snakes."

He states the fifth Rik:

Those who are in the trees, golden-yellow like young grass, blue-necked and red... || 5 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: Just as Rudras are situated in the worlds, they are also situated in the trees. Some of them are 'saspinjarah', having the golden-yellow color of young grass. 'Nilagrivah', having blue-colored necks. Some have blue color in the neck region. Others are 'vilohitah', especially red in color. Those who are of this nature (the rest is as before). || 5 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The fifth mantra. Those Rudras who are in the trees—either in the trees themselves or in all moving and stationary beings. Yellow like young grass. Those who age/destroy the tormentors of the world. Blue-necked. Specially red or free from impurity. Those who are superior as owners and protectors. (The rest is as before). || 5 ||

He states the sixth Rik:

Those who are the lords of spirits, shaven-headed or with matted hair... || 6 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
82                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

[Mantra 9:]
Obeisance to Rohita (the red-colored one), to the Sthapati (the lord-who-stands), to the Lord of trees — obeisance.

Now the ninth [commentary]: — "Namo rohitaya" (Obeisance to Rohita). "Rohita" means the one of red (lohita) color; "sthapati" means the lord who both stands (sthata) and protects (pata) — [this is] a word of the prshodaradi class (a grammatical class of irregularly formed words). Obeisance to such a one who is the Lord and master of trees — this is the meaning. Here the word "sthapati" conveys the reason for His lordship over trees: it is fitting that He who dwells within trees and protects them should be their lord. The word "rohita" (red) befits the Supreme Lord as the presiding deity of trees, for it will be stated later: "They who in trees are yellow-green, blue-necked, and deep red." Thus ends the ninth.

[Mantra 10:]
Obeisance to the wise counsellor (mantrin), to the merchant (vanija), to the Lord of thickets — obeisance.

Now the tenth [commentary]: — "Mantrine" (to the wise counsellor). The dense growths of forest-shrubs and the like are called "kakshas" (thickets); obeisance to their Lord — this is the primary meaning. But [one might object]: How can the Lord exercise sovereignty over what is exceedingly dense and impenetrable? Addressing this doubt about the impenetrability [of thickets], the text specifies with the word "vanijaya" (to the merchant): A body of merchants is called "vanija"; just as a merchant comprehends and makes clear all the dealings of a country, so too this [Lord] comprehends all things — nothing is impenetrable to Him — such is the intent. Another reason for His lordship over thickets is given

८२                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।
---

नमो रोहिताय स्थपतये वृक्षाणां पतये
नमः ॥

अथ नवमः— नमो रोहितायेति । लोहितवर्णो रो-
हितः, स्थाता चाक्षो पाता चेति स्थपतिः, पृषोदरादिः;
एवंभूताय वृक्षाणां पतये स्वामिने नमः इत्यर्थः । अत्र
स्थपतिशब्देन वृक्षस्वामित्वे हेतुरुच्यते, वृक्षेषु स्थित्वा
वृक्षान्पालयतस्तत्स्वामित्वौचित्यात् । रोहितशब्देन वृक्षाधि-
ष्ठातुः परमेश्वरस्य स्वरूपयुक्तं भवति, 'ये वृक्षेषु सस्पिञ्जरा
नीलाग्रीवा विलोहिताः' इति हि वक्ष्यते । इति नवमः ॥

नमो मन्त्रिणे वाणिजाय कक्षाणां प-
तये नमः ॥

अथ दशमः— मन्त्रिण इति । वनगुल्मादयोऽतिगहन-
पदार्थाः कक्षाः तेषां स्वामी कक्षाणां पतिः, तस्मै नमः इति
प्रथममन्वयः; नतु अतिगहनेषु कथमीश्वरस्याधिपत्यं स्या-
त्? दुरवगाह्त्वादित्याशङ्कय विशिनष्टि— वाणिजायेति।
वणिजां समूहो वाणिजः, यथा वणिक् सर्वजनपद्व्यवहारा-
न्परिच्छिद्य विशदीकरोति, तथा अयमपि सकलवस्तुजाव-
मिति, नास्य किञ्चिदुरवगाहमस्तीति भावः । केषुवि-

sanskrit.krama: ये भू॒ताना᳚म् । भू॒ताना॒मधि॑पतयः । अधि॑पतयो विशि॒खासः॑ । विशि॒खासः॑ कप॒र्दिनः॑ । कप॒र्दिन॒ इति॑ कप॒र्दिनः॑ ॥

translations.sayana:
[Na. E. A.] Rudradhyaya | 113

Those who are the lords (adhipatayah) of spirits (bhutanam), some shaven-headed (vishikhasah), some with matted hair (kapardinah). || 6 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: The word 'bhuta' refers to classes of followers who remain invisible and cause trouble to humans. The Rudras who are the lords of them—some are shaven-headed (vishikhasah), others have matted hair (kapardinah). || 6 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The sixth mantra. Those Rudras who are the lords of the five great elements or of the groups of followers; who are the causes of activity and withdrawal, and who reside in the seasons. 'Vishikhasah' means without hair (shikha) or with various types of hair or forms. 'Kapardinah' means those wearing crowns of matted hair. (Grammatical notes). || 6 ||

He states the seventh Rik:

Those who strike people through the food and through the vessels while they drink... || 7 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: Those Rudras who, residing secretly in the food (anneshu) being eaten, strike (vividhyanti) people especially by causing imbalance of humors etc. Similarly, residing secretly in the vessels (patreshu) in liquids like milk or water while they are being drunk, they strike people. || 7 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The seventh. Those Rudras who, residing in the food being eaten like cooked rice etc., strike people. They torment sinful men in various ways according to their sins through diseases etc. caused by imbalance of humors. Residing in the vessels of liquids like milk etc., they strike those who drink (pibatah). 'Drinking' is an illustration. Those residing in food strike the eaters, and those in vessels strike the drinkers. Or those who reside in vessels and cups. (The rest is as before). || 7 ||

For these three, the Purashcharana and everything else is as before.

He states the eighth Rik:

Those who are the protectors of the paths, the providers of food, the fighters... || 8 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
154                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

    ye tīrthāni pracaranti sṛkāvanto ni-
    ṣaṅgiṇaḥ ||

    Those Rudras who roam the sacred fords (tīrthāni) —
    bearing fangs (sṛkāvantaḥ) and swords (niṣaṅgiṇaḥ) —

Now the ninth mantra — beginning "ye tīrthāni" (those at the sacred fords). Those who bear fangs (sṛkāvantaḥ, equipped with sṛkvaṇīs — this being a Vedic lengthening of the vowel for Chandas) and who are sword-bearers (niṣaṅgiṇaḥ, carrying swords) — those Rudras who roam about the sacred fords such as the Gaṅgā and others — some to grant the fruit of the pilgrimage (tīrtha-phala) to certain devotees, and to withhold it from others — for them [the formula applies].

From "ye pathām" to "ye tīrthāni" — these two verses form a single mantra. The seer of this mantra is Devañca. The meter is Anuṣṭubh. The deity is Rudra. This mantra is primarily for granting the fruit of successful undertakings (subhna-phala). Mantra-siddhi is attained by reciting five thousand times, preceded by performance of the Kṛcchra (purificatory observance).

All of the preceding — beginning with "sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ" — are stated under a single [heading], with all particulars gathered together.

    ya etāvantaśca sūryāṃsaśca diśo
    rudrā vitasthire | teṣāṃ sahasrayojane 'va
    dhanvāni tanmasi ||

    As many Rudras as these, and as many as the rays of the sun,
    who have spread in all directions (diśo vitasthire) —
    their bows we unstring at a distance of a thousand yojanas.

Now the tenth mantra — beginning "ya etāvantaḥ" (as many as these). "Beginning with 'sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ'" — as many Rudras as have been mentioned up to this point — these many (etāvantaḥ, this number), and beyond them, even more numerous than the rays of the sun (sūryāṃsaḥ, like the countless rays of the sun) — those who are innumerable (beyond count) and presently existing — those Rudras who have spread out and pervaded all the ten directions (diśo vitasthire, stationed in every direction) — for them: "teṣāṃ sahasra-yojane ava dhanvāni tanmasi" (their bows we unstring at a distance of a thousand yojanas). The seer and other particulars here are as before.

translations.sayana:
[Na. E. A.] Rudradhyaya | 119

Those who roam in the holy places (tirthani) like Kashi, Prayaga, etc., to protect them. What kind of Rudras? 'Srikavanta': 'Srika' is a sharp-pointed weapon like a knife held in the hand. Others are 'Nishangina', holding swords. (The rest as before). || 9 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The ninth. Those Rudras who roam in the holy places to grant the fruit of pilgrimage to some and block others. Holding weapons (srikavanta) and swords (nishangina). (Grammatical notes). || 9 ||

[Purashcharana for these two (8 and 9)]: Having performed a Kricchra penance, chant the mantra 5,000 times to complete the Purashcharana. On the day of intended conception (ritu-gamana), sacrifice 8,000 offerings of cow-milk mixed with ghee in the fire; then perform the act. A learned and lineage-continuing son will be born. For going to a great pilgrimage, sacrifice 10,000 offerings of pure rice smeared with ghee. If one cannot reach the pilgrimage, having bathed and come back, worship the fire and keep silence; he obtains the fruit of the pilgrimage. Meditation etc. as before.

He states the tenth Rik:

Those Rudras who are so many, and even more, who have pervaded all directions... || 10 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: Those Rudras who are 'etavantah'—as many as mentioned in the previous Riks—and 'bhuyansah'—even more than that—who have pervaded (vitasthire) all directions. (The rest as before). Thus the ten Riks are stated. || 10 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    155
---

    namo rudrabhyo ye pṛthivyāṃ ye 'ntarikṣe
    ye divi yeṣām annaṃ vāto varṣim aṣavas te-
    bhyo daśa prācīṃ dik dakṣiṇā daśa pratī-
    cīṃ diśo udīcīṃ diśo dhvāṃs tebhyo namas te no
    mṛḍayantu te yaṃ dviṣmo yaśca no dveṣṭi taṃ
    vo jagme dadhāmi ||

    Salutation to the Rudras who [dwell] on earth, in the atmosphere,
    in the heavens — whose arrows are food, wind, and rain.
    To them, ten [salutations] toward the east, ten toward the south,
    ten toward the west, ten toward the north, ten upward —
    salutation to them! May they bring joy to us.
    That which we hate and that which hates us —
    that I place into your open mouth, O Rudras.

Now, by these mantras beginning "namo rudrabhyo ye pṛthivyām" — having bowed to the Rudras stationed in earth, atmosphere, and the heavenly worlds — the devotee prays for his own happiness and for the suffering of his enemies. The mantra is to be divided into three: "namo rudrabhyo ye pṛthivyāṃ yeṣām anniṣavaḥ tebhyo daśa prācīṃ dik ... dadhāmi" is the first mantra. "Namo rudrabhyo ye 'ntarikṣe yeṣāṃ vāta iṣavaḥ tebhyo daśa prācīṃ dik ... dadhāmi" is the second mantra. "Namo rudrabhyo ye divi yeṣāṃ varṣim aṣavaḥ tebhyo daśa prācīṃ dik ... dadhāmi" is the third mantra.

The meaning of the first verse: Salutation (namaḥ) be to the Rudras who dwell on the earth — whose arrows (iṣavaḥ, instruments of smiting) are food (annam). [The meaning of] "arrows being food" is this: by causing heedless eating (apradhyāna-bhakṣaṇa) and excessive eating (atimātrāśana), they afflict some with disease; they torment others by destroying their wealth and causing hunger; they cause others yet to commit theft and such crimes for the sake of wealth, leading to punishment including death — thus food is their arrow, the instrument of their harm against sinners. To those Rudras, I make ten folded-hand salutations (añjalīḥ) facing the east (prācīḥ, eastward-facing salutations) — with all ten fingers spread out together. In making añjalīs (cupped-hand salutations) while facing east, ten such salutations come from facing east. The same is to be understood for [salutations facing] the south and the other directions. Thus I perform salutations in all directions with full folded hands — this is the meaning. By this, the bodily salutation (kāya-namaskāra) is shown; "tebhyo namaḥ" (salutation to them) shows the mental salutation; and "namo rudrabhyo" —

श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                    १५५
---

लौकिकवैदिकमार्गरक्षका इत्यर्थः । एवदेव रक्षकत्वं
विशेषण्द्वयेनोपपादयति— ऐलबुदा यव्युघ इति । इ-
शाशब्दोऽश्वाची, इराणां समूहे ऐरम्, येरमेव ऐलम्, तद्वि-
भ्रतीति ऐलवृतः; ते एव ऐलवृदाः; भकारस्य बकारः त-
कारस्य द्त्वम् अकारस्य वीर्घश्चान्दसः अन्नदाता इत्यर्थः ।
यविति मिश्रीसवित पापेनेति यः पापिष्ठः, युमिर्युष्यन्तीति
यव्युघः, पूर्वेवदेव छान्दसी रूपनिष्पत्तिः । पापिष्ठानां निरो-
द्धार इत्यर्थः । एवं च लौकिकवैदिकमार्गरक्षकत्वं नाम सत्पु-
दषाम् तेषु मार्गेषु प्रवळ्ये तेषां तत्कलेरञ्चाचेः संयोजनम्,
पापिष्ठानां तत्र निरोधनमित्येवंरूपमिति भावः । एताटशा
ये रुद्राः, तेषाम् ॥

    ये तीर्थानि प्रचरन्ति सृकावन्तो नि-
षङ्गिणः ॥

तीर्थानि गङ्गादीनि रक्षितुं प्रचरन्ति केषांचित्तीर्थफलं दातुं
केषांचित्तनिरोधं च, तेषाम् । अत्र ये 'पथां ये तीर्थानीति

रुद्रो देवता । सुघ्नफलप्रदोऽयं मन्त्रः । कुच्छ्राचरणपूर्वेकं

translations.sayana:
[Na. E. A.] Rudradhyaya | 119

Those who roam in the holy places (tirthani) like Kashi, Prayaga, etc., to protect them. What kind of Rudras? 'Srikavanta': 'Srika' is a sharp-pointed weapon like a knife held in the hand. Others are 'Nishangina', holding swords. (The rest as before). || 9 ||

Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya: The ninth. Those Rudras who roam in the holy places to grant the fruit of pilgrimage to some and block others. Holding weapons (srikavanta) and swords (nishangina). (Grammatical notes). || 9 ||

[Purashcharana for these two (8 and 9)]: Having performed a Kricchra penance, chant the mantra 5,000 times to complete the Purashcharana. On the day of intended conception (ritu-gamana), sacrifice 8,000 offerings of cow-milk mixed with ghee in the fire; then perform the act. A learned and lineage-continuing son will be born. For going to a great pilgrimage, sacrifice 10,000 offerings of pure rice smeared with ghee. If one cannot reach the pilgrimage, having bathed and come back, worship the fire and keep silence; he obtains the fruit of the pilgrimage. Meditation etc. as before.

He states the tenth Rik:

Those Rudras who are so many, and even more, who have pervaded all directions... || 10 ||

Sayanacharya Bhashya: Those Rudras who are 'etavantah'—as many as mentioned in the previous Riks—and 'bhuyansah'—even more than that—who have pervaded (vitasthire) all directions. (The rest as before). Thus the ten Riks are stated. || 10 ||
translations.abhinava_shankara:
---
156                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

— the "namaḥ" (salutation) at the beginning of the mantra is the verbal salutation (vācika). Thus we who bow with all three faculties (body, mind, and speech) — that which we hate (yaṃ dviṣmaḥ) and that which hates us (yaś ca no dveṣṭi) — that enemy, who embodies our sinful deeds and their fruits and the instruments thereof — O Rudras, into your open mouth (jaghme, the open jaws) we place (dadhāmi) him — meaning: destroy him. The same connection applies to the second and third mantras. The Rudras who dwell in the atmosphere (antarikṣe) — whose arrows are wind (vāta, their arrow being a weapon of affliction): they afflict sinners by producing various diseases through wind-disorders, and through generating violent storms and the like — this is the meaning. The Rudras who dwell in the heavens (divi) — whose arrows are rain (varṣima, rain-related) — by excessive rainfall, drought, and the like they afflict sinners — the rest is to be understood everywhere as it pleases. The entire collection of yajus (Yajurvedic verses) beginning with "namo rudrabhyaḥ" forms a single mantra. Here the deity and seer are both Rudra. The meditation verse is the same as what was stated under "sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ" — that is to be used here as well. This mantra is primarily for warding off evil influences and harmful acts (añcamasya-di-prada). The method of puraścaraṇa (ritual practice for mantra-perfection), the variations in the results such as neutralizing hostile acts (kṛtyotpādana), pacification (śānti), subjugation (stambhana), and the like — these are to be found elaborated in the Rudra-Kalpa and other texts.

१५६                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।
---

पञ्चसहस्रजपातुरञ्चरणसिद्धिः । अन्यतस्सर्वेम् 'सहस्राणि
सहस्रशः' इत्येकोक्तम् ॥

    य ग्तावन्तश्च सूर्याꣳसश्च दिशो
रुद्रा वितस्थिरे । तेषाꣳ सहस्रयोजनेऽव
धन्वानि तन्मसि ॥

    अथ दशमः— य एतावन्त इति । 'सहस्राणि सह-
स्रशः' इत्यारभ्य यावन्तोऽमी रुद्रा उक्ताः एतावन्तश्च, इतो-
ऽपि सूर्यासः बहुतराः इयत्तामवीळ्य वर्तमानाः ये रुद्राः
दिशो रुद्रा वितस्थिरे सर्वां दिशो व्याप्य स्थिताः, तेषां
सहस्रयोजनेऽव धन्वानि तन्मसीति । अत्र ऋष्यादिकं
पूर्ववत् ॥

    नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां येऽन्तरिक्षे
ये दिवि येषामन्नं वातो वर्षिमषवस्ते-
भ्यो दश प्राचींदेश दक्षिणा दश प्रती-
चींदेशोदीचींदेशोध्वाꣳस्तेभ्यो नमस्ते नो
मृडयन्तु ते यं भिऋमो यञ्च नो देछि तं
वो जन्मे द्धामि ॥

    अथ 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां' इत्यादिभिर्मन्त्रैः
पृथिव्यन्तरिक्षुलोकास्थितान् रुद्राञ्मस्कृत्य स्वस्य सुखं क्षे-
विषणां दु:खं च प्रार्थयते । अग्रेऽथं मन्त्रत्रयविभागः— 'नमो
रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां येषामन्निषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदे-
श....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये अन्तरिक्षे
येषां बात इषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इति द्वि-
तीयो मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये दिवि येषां वर्षिमषवः
तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । तत्र
प्रथमखायसर्थः— नमो रुद्रेभ्यो अस्तु ये पृथिव्यां वर्त-
न्ते, येषां च अन्नम् इषवः इषुस्तानीयं हिंसायाचवनम्;
अपध्यानभक्षणम् अतिमात्राशनं च कारयित्वा तेन रोगा-
तुल्तय काञ्चिद्धाघन्ते, काञ्चिद्धनविघातेन क्षुधमुल्तय पीड-
यन्ति, काञ्चिद्धनार्थे चौर्यादि कारयित्वा वधाधिकं प्राणयन्ति;
एवं पापिष्ठहिंसायाम् अञ्चम् निमित्तामिति तदेव एषामिषवः ।
तेभ्यो रुद्रेभ्यो दश भाचीः प्राञ्जुखाखिलरूपा अञ्जुलीः
करोमि । प्राञ्जुखत्वेन अञ्जलीकरणे हि दशाञ्जुलयः प्रागभा-
भवन्ति । एवं दक्षिणाभुभ्वान्तेषु दृष्टव्याः । ईदशाखिलोरू-
पाञ्जुल्युपलाखिताञ्जमस्कारान्करोमील्यर्थः । अनेन कायकन-
मस्कारो दर्शितः, तेभ्यो नम इति मानसः, नमो रुद्रेभ्यो

translations.sayana:
[Na. Dvi. Am.] Rudradhyaya. 31

...the word 'jagat' is formed thus. [Grammatical note]. The collective meaning is: he who makes beings weary in worldly existence (Samsara) and also protects the weary. Some say: having described the five Brahmans separately with the first five mantras (1-5), the sixth mantra describes the God who is the destroyer of Samsara and the Lord of cattle (beings). (6)

He speaks the seventh Yajus:
Salutation to Rudra who protects with a drawn bow, salutation to the lord of fields. (7)

[Word-for-word]: Namah | rudraya | atatavine | iti | ata-tavine | kshetranam | pataye | namah || 7 ||

Sayana's Commentary: 'Atatavi' is he who protects (avati) with an 'atata'—a drawn bow. Salutation be to that Rudra. Salutation be to Rudra who is the protector of fields (kshetra). (7)

Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya: 'Namo rudraya'—explained already. 'Atatavine'—he who protects the worlds with an 'atata'—a bow on which the string is fixed; or he who holds such a bow. [Grammatical note on the word 'atatavine']. 'Kshetranam pataye namah'—to the protector or Master of the 'kshetras' (bodies). For instance:
"The Lord, O Arjuna, resides in the heart-region of all beings, making all beings revolve by his power (maya) as if mounted on a machine."
Or: to the Master of holy places (punya-kshetra) like Avimukta (Kashi) etc. Or: to him in the form of the protector of holy fields. The idea is: salutation to the cutter and promoter of Samsara. (7)

He speaks the eighth Yajus:
Salutation to the charioteer, to the one who cannot be harmed, salutation to the lord of forests. (8)

[Word-for-word]: Namah | sutaya | ahantyaya | vananam | pataye | namah || 8 ||

Sayana's Commentary: 'Sutah' is the charioteer (sarathi). 'Ahantyaya'—to him who is incapable of being killed (ahantya) by enemies. Salutation be to such a Rudra. Salutation be to Rudra who is the protector of forests (vana). (8)

Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya: 'Namah sutaya'. 'Sutah' is the charioteer, the controller; by being the controller of the chariot of the world-machine. Or: 'Sutah' is the narrator of the Puranas, in his position at the beginning of the Yugas. Or...
...
translations.abhinava_shankara:
80                    Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam

---

    "Salutation to the weapon (heti) of Bhava (Śiva the cause of existence); salutation to the Lord of the worlds (jagatāṃ pataye namaḥ)."

Thus through five mantras, the Lord has been saluted with demonstrations of his lordship over certain specific things such as the directions and so forth. Now, since it is impossible to salute him individually in respect of his sovereignty over each and every thing, the sixth mantra is employed to salute him as the "Lord of the worlds" (jagatpati): "Namo bhavasya iti" — Bhava means saṃsāra (the cycle of existence); the heti (weapon) which cuts it — meaning he is the one who severs the stream of worldly existence (saṃsāravṛtta). He is the lord (pati) of all the worlds (jagatāṃ), both moving and unmoving. To him may there be salutation. Although the lordship over the entire world, from Brahmā to the stationary, was already stated by [the mantra] "Paśūnāṃ pataye namaḥ" — [the difference is that] there, it was stated in the sense of his being the cause of the bondage of saṃsāra; here, the distinction is that he is the liberator from saṃsāra. Both aspects, however, are attributes of Brahman.

    "Salutation to Rudra (the one who dispels affliction), to the one who stretches the bow (ātatāvitane), to the lord of the fields (kṣetrāṇāṃ pataye namaḥ)."

Now, to further support what has already been said about his lordship over the world, and in order to salute the Blessed Lord again through [the demonstration of] his sovereignty over various particular things — in the manner of a persistent supplicant walking again and again — the commentary begins the seventh [mantra] and those following: "Namo Rudrāya iti" — Rudra is he who dispels (drāvaka) the suffering of saṃsāra; hence, because he protects (avati, i.e., rakṣati) with the stretched (ātatena) bow, he is Ātatāvitin. Kṣetrāṇi means bodies (śarīrāṇi), as the Smṛti says: "This body, O son of Kuntī, is called the kṣetra (the field)" — he who is the lord of these bodies is kṣetrāṇāṃ patiḥ, to him may there be salutation. Just as in ordinary life the inhabitants of a land are protected by the overlord of that land —

commentaries_sanskrit.abhinava_shankara:
८०                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।

    नमो भवस्य हेत्यै जगतां पतये नम: ॥

   इत्थं पञ्चभिर्मन्त्रैदिंगादिकतिपयवस्त्वाधिपत्यप्रदर्शनपूर्व-
कमीश्वरो नमस्कृत:; इदानीं प्रतिवस्त्वाघिपत्यवचनपूर्वक-
नमस्कारस्याशक्यतया जगत्पतित्वेन तं नमस्कर्तुं षष्ठो मन्त्र:
भयुक:— नमो भवस्येति । भव: संसार:, तस्य च्छेद्ने
हेति: आयुघम्, संसारवृत्तस्य च्छेत्तेत्यर्थ: । स्थावरजङ्गमानां
लोकानां स्वामी जगतां पति:, तस्मै नम: । यद्यपि
'पशूनां पतये नम:' इत्यनैव ब्रह्मादिस्थावरान्तसकलजग-
त्पतित्वमुक्तकम्, तथापि संसारपाशबन्धहेतुत्वेन तदुक्तकम्;
अत्र संसारमोचकत्वेनेति भेद:, उभयोरपि ब्रह्मघर्मत्वात् ॥

    नमो रुद्रायाततावितने क्षेत्राणां पतये
नम: ॥

   अथ उक्तमेव जगत्पतित्वसुपलपादयितुं श्रमाबधि घाव-
न्न्यायेन पुनरपि तत्तद्वस्त्वाघिपत्येन भगवन्तं नमस्कर्तुमारभे
सप्तमादिभि:— नमो रुद्रायेति । रुद्र: संसारदु:खद्रावक:,
अत एव आत्वज्येन धनुषा अवति रक्षतीति आततावितने;
क्षेत्राणि शरीराणि, 'इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते'
इति स्मृते:, तेषां स्वामी क्षेत्राणां पति:, तस्मै नम: इत्य-
र्थ: । लोके हि देशाधिपतिकलत्रपद्रेभ्यो देशवासिनो रक्षित,

rudradhyaya.sanskrit:
[न॰ए॰अ॰] रुद्राध्यायः । १२७

आदावङ्गानि संयोज्य स्नात्वा देहे स्वके द्विजः ।
ततो वत्स महादेवं पूजयेद्विधिपूर्वकम् ॥
एकं लिङ्गं नदीतीरे ह्यपरास्यं जलैः स्नपेत् ।
पञ्चगव्येन भूयोऽपि शंभुं पलशतेन च ॥
संस्नाप्य वारिणा भूयो ध्यात्वा तं जगदीश्वरम् ।

( एकं लिङ्गं दृषभादिपरिवाररहितम् । अपरास्यं पश्चिमाभिमुखम् )
तद्वोरोचनया सार्धं लेपयेद्भक्तितो हरम् ॥
श्रीइक्षोत्यैस्ततः पत्रैर्नैनिशिंछद्रैर्जैन्तुवजितैः ।
नैव पर्युपितैः किंशुधैः प्रोक्षितैरर्चयेच्छिवम् ॥
एकादश दिनान्येवमपर्चयेत्तं महेश्वरम् ।
विधिनाऽनेन देवेशं भूयस्तं वै निवोध मे ॥

दिनान्येकादशैवाऽऽदौ पूजान्ते होममाचरेत् ।
साञ्यैर्विश्वदलैर्नित्यं दक्षिणां दिशमास्थितः ॥
एवं नीलोत्पलैर्भृपः पञ्चैशैव सुगन्धिभिः ।
होममचों मकुर्वांत त्रिरेकादशवासरान् ॥
सघृतं गुग्गुलुं दग्ध्वा नित्यमेव हरिमयम् ।
पूजान्ते परया भक्त्या तस्यैवं धनसंचयः ।
वित्तकामो यदा सोऽथ स्यादालिङ्क्य वनस्पतीन् ।
जपेद्रुद्राञ्छिवं ध्यायेद्दृष्ट्वं ते हैमतां ययुः ॥

सपुष्पं वा तरुं ब्राह्मं विल्वं षाऽऽलिङ्क्य ताञ्जपेत् ।
आवर्तयन्वह्नुनवारान्स दक्षो हैमतां व्रजेत् ॥
तीर्थे कनखले गत्वा जप्त्वा रुद्रान्वह्नुन्पुनः ।
जपेन्मध्यजले कृष्णपाषाणा हैमतामियुः ॥
महापर्वतमाश्रित्य अवष्टभ्य दिने दिने ।
जपेदेकादशाहान्ते निधिं प्राप्नोति पुष्कलम् ॥
समिधो वैतसीश्वैताभिमध्वक्ता जुहोति यः ।
तदन्ते जायते तस्य द्राविणं पुष्कलं बहु ॥
मधुबिल्वादयो दक्षा आलिङ्क्यैकादशे दिने ।
जपान्ते हैमतां यान्ति तथ्यं तत्र न संशयः ॥

१ यजुः इति पाठान्तरम् ।

CC 0- Jangamwadi Math Collection. Digitized by eGangotri
१२८ सायणाचार्य–भट्टभास्करभाष्यसहितः— [न०५०अ०]

अथवा लिङ्गमाश्रित्य स्त्रयभ्युपराङ्मुखम् ।
संछाथैकादशाहानि जपेदेतत्समुपङ्गजैः ॥
पशून्वा कुत्सितांश्चैव न पश्येच्च संस्पृशेत् ।
यं पश्येघ्पपयाते तु हेम स्याद्दादशेऽहनि ॥

अथान्यः—

कृष्णाष्टमीं समारभ्य यावत्कृष्णचतुर्दशी ।
स्थण्डिलं चोपलिप्याकमेस्तरे वा जपेत्सुधीः ॥

'नमो अस्तु नीलग्रीवाय' इत्यनेन सहस्राभिमन्त्रितं घृतपायसमीश्वराय
निवेदयेत् । काषापणानां सहस्रमुत्पद्यते । अथान्यः । 'प्रमुञ्च धन्वनः' इत्यनेन
यथासिद्धान्तं महानसेऽभावरुग्महस्रं जुहुयात् । द्रव्यमन्यवचिच्छन्नमक्षयं भवति ।
अथान्यः । 'नमो वः किरिकेभ्यः' इति तिलाहुतीनामयुतं जुहुयात् । काषा-
पणानां सहस्रमुत्पद्यते'' । इति काळिका पुराणे ।

अथाळकामस्य शौनकः—

'असौ यस्ताम्रः' इत्यनेनोभयेनाहरहरद्यास्तमययोरादित्यमुपतिष्ठेताक्षय-
ग्रमुपतिष्ठते ।

अथ बौधायनः —

उदुम्वरसमिभिर्भध्वक्षतैर्वां रुद्रमहारुद्रातिरुद्राणामन्यतमं जप्त्वा तच्चोक्त-
संख्यया जुहुयाद्रन्यमुत्पद्यते ।

अथ वृष्टिकामस्य शौनकः ।

'असौ यस्ताम्रः' इत्यनेनोदकशाकयवाहारो वेतससमिघामयुतं जुहुया-
ड्डक्षीराक्तानां वाऽयुतं जुहुयात् । भगवानादित्यो वृष्टिं मुञ्चति ।

अथायुष्कामस्य स एवाऽऽह—

आयुष्कामः पूर्वोक्तिविधिना देवमाराध्य सर्वदिक्षु रक्तपीतकृष्णबलीन्द्र-
त्वा रुद्रैकादशिनीमेकादशकृत्वो जप्त्वा 'आराचे गोत्र' इति षोडशोपचा-
रान्कृत्वा तमेव मन्त्रं सहस्रं जपेत् । व्याधितश्चेद्वेदिदिने ब्राह्मणाय दक्षिणां
दत्वा सहस्रं जापयेत् । व्याधिभ्यः प्रमुच्यते शतायुर्भवति ।

अथ पुत्रकामस्य शौनकः—

'परि णो रुद्रस्य' इति वटसमिधामयुतं जुहुयात्पुत्र आयुष्मानुत्पद्यते ।
अथ गोकामस्य स एव—

१ अत्र 'स्वयभ्वपरादिह्मुखद्' इति शुद्धः पाठः कल्पनीयः ।

CC.0- Jangamwadi Math Collection. Digitized by eGangotri
[न०प्०अ०] रुद्राध्यायः । १२९

' नमो वः किरिकेभ्यः ' इति मन्त्रेण वैकङ्कुतोदुम्वरसमिधां दध्यक्तानामयुतं जुहुयात् । अनन्तगोस्वामी भवति । न च गावः प्रक्षीयन्ते । अथवाऽनेनैव षष्ठेनाकेपणशतं गोपयसैकाद्शाऽऽहुतीजुहुयादेकादश गाव उत्पद्यन्ते ।

अथ वाकामस्य स एवाऽऽह—

महाचतुष्पथे वल्मीकमृदा लिङ्गं प्रतिष्ठाप्य पञ्चगव्येन स्नापयेत् । ' नमो षः किरिकेभ्यः ' इत्यनया चाभिमन्त्र्य ' मा नस्तोके ' इति घृतपायसं दिनद्वयं विश्वमुपहरेत् । दक्षिणामूर्तौ वचाफलसमर्थफलं चूर्णं सुसूक्ष्मं कृत्वा ताम्रभाजने न्यसेत् । कपिलाघृतेनाऽऽलोड्य मदेशिन्या नियुताभिमभ्र्छितं कृत्वा पिवेदमतिहतवाक्यो भवति ।

पुनः स एव—'द्रापे अन्धसस्पते' इत्यादिना शतसहस्रं जुहुयात् । वासांस्यक्षराणि भवन्ति । अथ वशकामस्य बोधायनः—

रुद्रैकादशिनीलघुरुद्रमहारुद्रातिरुद्रेष्वन्यतमं जप्त्वा
जुहुयाच्छ्रुतपुष्पाणि स विश्वं कुरुते वशम् ।
अथ कृत्स्नं जगत्क्रिड्दृशीकर्तुं समीहते ।
नाभिमात्रजलस्थश्च जपेद्रुद्रान्वद्दृंस्तथा ।।
कृत्वा चैवार्चनं शंभोरादाय नियमं ततः ।
आदित्याभिमुखो भूत्वा रुद्रानावर्तयेत्सदा ।।
तस्याऽऽवर्तयतो रुद्राञ्जिताक्षस्य च धीमतः ।
हृपकेतुः समायाति वरदो भक्तवत्सलः ।।

अथाऽऽरोग्यकामस्य शौनकः—' मा नो महान्तम् ' इति मन्त्रेण तिलाहुतीनामयुतं जुहुयात् । वालानां परिजनस्याऽऽरोग्यं भवति । अथ मत्युपस्थिते सर्वभये क्षशविमोक्षकामः पूर्वोक्तविधिना मन्त्रदेवन्यासादिकं कृत्वा षलीश द्रवा ' भीहुष्टम ' इति पुनः षोडशोपचारं कृत्वा रुद्रैकादशिनीमेकादशकृत्वो जप्त्वा जापयित्वा वा रुद्रवेदिनं सर्वेभ्यो भयेभ्यः प्रमुच्यते नात्र संशयः । ' इमाऽ रुद्राप ' इति मन्त्रेण दधिमधुपायसाहुतीनां मत्येकमयुतं जुहुयात्सर्वभयान्युपशाम्यन्ति ।

अथ शान्तिकामः पूर्वोक्तविधिना मन्त्रदेवन्यासादिकं कृत्वा देवमाराध्य रुद्रवेदिनं ब्राह्मणमाचार्यं हृत्वा स्वयंभूलिङ्गमभिगम्य तत्राप्यनेनैव विधिनाऽऽराघनं कृत्वा पञ्चामृतैः स्नापयित्वा प्रथमानुवाकेनाऽऽऽयहोमं कृत्वा देषं संपूज्य ' नमस्ते रुद्र मन्यवे ' इत्येकादशानुवाकानां जपं कुर्यात् । धारामास्नावर्य-

१ °मर्यंक° ।

१७
१३०          सायणाचार्ये–भट्टभास्करभाष्यसहितः—   [न०प०अ०]

स्तथा जपान्ते ' अग्नाविष्णू सजोषसा ' इत्येकादशानुवाकानामेकमेकं जपेत् ।
पुनः पञ्चामृतैः स्नापयेत्ततो देवं समभ्यच्येे भक्ष्यभोज्ययुतं नैवेद्यं दत्वा चतुरः
पूर्णकलशान्पञ्चमृत्तिकापञ्चपल्लवपञ्चरत्नयुतानक्षतगन्धधूपादिभिर्चेयित्वा सित-
षट्वेष्टितान्कृत्वा ' शं नो वातः पवताम् ' इति शान्तिं ध्यायञ्जप्त्वाऽभिषेकं
कारयेच्छान्तिर्भवति । ततः स्वस्थो भवति । आचार्याय सितवस्त्रयुगलं धेनुं
काञ्चनं च दद्याद्ब्राह्मणांश्च भोजयेच्छान्तिर्भवति । पुनः शौनकः—'मा
नो महान्तम्' इति मन्त्रेणाश्वत्यसमिधामयुतं जुहुयात् । प्रतिसंवत्सरं यात्रा-
यामयुतं जुहुयात् । एवं कृत आत्मीयस्य बालस्वीतद्धजातस्य शान्तिः । 'नमो
भवाय ' 'नमो ज्येष्ठाय ' इत्येताभ्यां कुमारग्रहादिग्रस्तानां भस्माभि-
मन्त्र्य रक्षां कारयेत् । रात्रौ शिखां च वध्नीयात्कुमारादिग्रहभयं न भवति ।
' मीढुष्टम शिवतम ' इति महामये चौरभयेऽग्निभये प्राणात्यये वा मनसा
जपेत्क्षेमेणाऽऽगच्छति । ' इमाख् रुद्राय ' इत्यनेन सुवर्णेद्वांङ्कुरैः शतसहस्रं
जुहुयात् । महाज्वरोपघाते गतायुरपि दीर्घायुर्भवति । ' नमस्ते रुद्रमन्यवे '
इति चतुर्भिः प्रत्यृचं यवगोधूमगव्वीघुकजतिलानां दधिमधुघृताक्तानामयुतं जुहु-
यात् । एकादशद्वादशरात्रेण वा वायुमुपशमयति । ' अध्यवोचद्घिवक्ता '
इति सर्वरक्षा कर्तव्या । आतुरेभ्यः सर्पेपानसप्ताभिमन्त्रितान्कृत्वा चतुर्दिशं
प्रक्षिपेद्दशदिक्षु रक्षा कृता भवति । पथि गच्छतोऽसच्छान्तिमुपगच्छति । सर्पे-
पेण ब्रह्मराक्षसभूतपिशाचादिभ्यो मोक्षयति । ' या ते रुद्र शिवा तनूः '
इति ऋग्याऽन्तिपा तया प्रतिवाऽसरं सहस्राभिमन्त्रितं कृत्वैकादश ग्रन्थी-
न्दर्वा बालानां गुर्विणीनां च हस्ते वध्नीयात् । बाला गुर्विण्यश्च सुखेन वर्धन्ते ।
यक्षराक्षसाञ्चुत्सादनं च । ' सहस्राणि ' इति रक्तवाससा रक्तोष्णीपरक्त-
माल्यानुलेपनेदेवं समचेयेत् । इमशान एकतुक्षे नदीपुलिने चतुष्पथे वने वा
कार्पासतण्डुलपरिवेष्टितां महातैलाक्तां इमशानाशौ मद्दीप्तेन यवपिष्टनैकादशा-
भिमन्त्रितेन यक्षराक्षसादेः शत्रोरपि प्रतिकृतिं कृत्वोरसि कटुतैलेन प्रतिमां
कृत्वाऽऽस्थाप्य दक्षिणभूमिभाग एकेकेन शरेणैकादशप्राणायतनेषु विंतो-
दयेत् । सहस्मांगतं सकलळोकगतं वा शत्रुमुत्सादयति । ' नमो रुद्रेभ्यः '
इति मध्याह्ने रुत्रमकण्टकेऽजुहुयात्सर्पेपेश्वाहरद्रवों मत्यापत्तीं राष्ट्रे देवयो-
नयः सपिशाचाश्च भ्रंशन्ति खेचरीणां ह्लीणां यातुधानानां वा भस्मी
भवन्ति । अथेमाः शान्तयः—' इमाख् रुद्राय ' इति मन्त्रेण
गवामुपघाते गोष्ठमध्येऽग्निमुपसमाधाय वैकङ्गतसमिधामयुतं जुहुयात् ।
गवां शान्तिर्भवति । यावन्तश्चतुष्पदास्तावञ्चय एवमेव शान्तिविधि

१ वारमिति पाठान्तरम् ।
[न०प०ञ०] रुद्राध्यायः १. १३१

कुर्यात् । "नमस्ते रुद्र मन्यवे" इत्यनेन गत्रामुपघाते गोष्ठमध्येऽग्निमु-
पसमाधाय वैकङ्कतसमिधामयुतं जुहुयात् । पश्वादीनां शान्तिद्वैगुणेन महा-
शान्तिरिति ।

अथ शत्रुमारणकामः पूर्वोक्तविधिना मन्त्रन्यासादि कृत्वा दिक्पालेभ्यो
रक्तौदनबलीन्दत्वा 'स्तुहि श्रुतम्' इत्यनेन षोडशोपचारान्कृत्वा रुद्रै-
कादशिनीमेकादशकृत्वो जपित्वा पुनः 'स्तुहि श्रुतम्' इति सहस्रं जपेत्पत्य-
हम् । सप्तरात्रेण शत्रुविनाशो भवति । अथ कृत्यामुत्पादयितुकामो रक्तवाससा
रक्तोष्णीषरक्तमाल्यानुलेपनेन्देत्रमर्चयेत् । श्मशानाङ्कणे लोहितशलाकाः भादे-
शपात्रा महातैलाक्ता लक्षं जुहुयात् । "उत्तिष्ठ रक्ताङ्गि रक्तनेत्रे रक्तकेशि"
कन्यां तां क्षिप्रमेव रुधिरकुम्भेन पूर्णमांसमात्रेण तर्पयेत् । अन्यथा कर्तारे-
मेव हिंसेत् । सा त्वरिता यत्निता वदति "कं घर्षयामि वद वा ज्ञायते यदि
त्रैलोक्यस्थितमभ्युत्सादायिष्यामि" इति । तथा चैकैकस्य शरीरस्य ग्रामे
नगरे गृहे च प्रयोगः । शाकं यशस्यमुपयोक्ष्यमुपोष्य द्वादशरात्राणि लघुभ्यो
लघुनि महान्तीति अमह्दीनानि तुलापुरुषचान्द्रायणानां मध्यम एव त्रिकल्प्य
ग्रहणे त्वाचम्य नित्याभिवाद्यावलम्बिनाऽन्यथा पठेत्, इति। 'प्रमुञ्च धन्वनः'
इति षड्भिरायुधमाभिमन्त्र्य सङ्ग्रामोपनीताः शत्रवः पलायन्ते। अभिमुखा नाव-
तिष्ठन्ते । दुर्भेकण्टकानामयुतं जुहुयात् । प्रभूतः शत्रूयते क्षत्रियादिः । क्षत्रियो
दिवसत्रिभागेन वैद्यः षड्भागेन चतुर्भागेन शूद्रः । तथा चाव्याहतं कर्मेत्याच-
क्षते । 'त्रिकिरिद त्रिलोहित' इति यथा शत्रुभिरनेकपर्यायसुप्तशत्रुमुखे जुहु-
यात् । सर्वे विभग्रमनसो विमुखाः शत्रवश्च पलायन्ते । 'नमो हिरण्यवाहवे'
इत्यनुवाकैः कटुतैलाक्तानां राजाभिशङ्कितानां सर्पेणाञ्जुहुयात् । चक्रमुत्साद-
यति। फट्कारेण देवस्योपरि मालामधोशिरा(?)पवलम्ब्य सशिरसा वस्त्रेण रुधि-
रमाञ्चाय त्रिकटुकेनाभ्यज्य देवकराङ्गुलिना रेखापुत्पाद्य शत्रोर्नामानुस्मरञ्ज-
पेत् । सबलसमुदायं शत्रुपुञ्चाटयति । एवं कृत्स्नं ग्राममुञ्चाटयति एकाह्ना ।
राजानमहोरात्रेण संपूर्णमर्धदिवसेन क्षत्रियं क्षपयति । दिवसत्रिभागेन वैद्यं
चतुर्भागेण शूद्रम् । तथा चाव्याहतं कर्मेत्याचक्षते । एतत्सर्वे शौनकोक्तं
हेमाद्रौ । अथ मेरुतन्त्रोक्तम्—

"रौद्रीं धारां ततः कुर्यात्मोच्यते तद्विधानकम् ।
नमस्ते रुद्राशाविष्णु इत्यध्यायद्रूपेण च ॥
आवर्तैनमिति मोक्तं गङ्गाञ्जानफलप्रदम् ।
आवर्तैनाभिषेकं तु यः कुर्यात्मत्यहं नरः ।।

१ अन्नह्दीनानि इति पाठान्तरम् ।
१३२ सायणाचार्य–भट्टभास्करभाष्यसहितः— [न०प०अ०]

तस्यैव दिनजं पापं दैवजं नाशमाप्नुयात् ।
संध्यावन्दनतः क्षीणं तच्छेत्माग्जन्मजं भवेत् ॥
एकः पाठो नमस्तेऽस्य ह्यनुवाकः परस्य च ।
एवं भवाय नमस्तेऽस्याघ्राविष्णू तथैककम् ॥
इयं रुद्री समाख्याता रौद्रपापनिकृन्तनी ।
अनया प्रत्यहं यस्तु कुर्याच्छिञ्जांभिषेचनम् ॥
जन्मजन्मकृतं पापं वर्षपद्ये प्रणइयति ।
रुद्रीभिरेकादशभिर्लघुरुद्रः प्रकीर्तितः ॥
अनेन सिक्तं यैर्लिङ्गं ते न पश्यन्ति भास्करिम् ।
एकादशभिरेतैस्तु महारुद्रः प्रकीर्तितः ॥
निर्धनो धनमाप्नोति महापातकनाशनः ।
अनेन विहितो होमः सोमयागफलप्रदः ॥
अनधिकारी दीक्षायां करे पक्षत्रयं मतः ।
एकादशमहारुद्रैरतिरुद्रः प्रकीर्तितः ॥
अतिपापहरो यस्माद्दृष्टाऽन्या नैव निष्कृतिः" ॥
इति मेरुतन्त्रोक्तं विधानं समाप्नुम् ॥
इति श्रीभट्टभास्करविरचिते यजुर्वेदभाष्येऽग्निकाण्डे श्रीरुद्रप्रश्नसंज्ञितः
पञ्चमः प्रपाठकः समाप्तः ।
॥ समाप्तमिदं नपकभाष्यम् ॥
