rudradhyaya.english:
[Rudradhyaya Rituals Continued]

(Continuation of Shaunaka/Sanatkumara methods):
"The learned man who chants the Shatarudriya in the morning, noon, and evening with a pure mind, attains the fruit of all sacrifices. He is freed from all sins and becomes one with Rudra. The king who performs this ritual annually with eleven Brahmins, as described, attains universal sovereignty and his kingdom is free from all troubles."

(The text seems to end here or transition to concluding remarks).
Thus ends the Bhashya on the Rudradhyaya.
Salutations to the Supreme Soul.
Om Tat Sat.

128 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. E. A.]

Alternatively, taking refuge in a Linga that faces the West (svayambhv-apara-mukham), and keeping silence for eleven days, chant this along with the supplementary parts. One should not see or touch domestic animals or vile persons. If one happens to see them, the object will turn to gold on the twelfth day.

Another method:
Starting from the 8th day of the dark fortnight until the 14th day, having smeared the sacrificial ground (sthandila) or a mat, the wise one should chant. Offering 1,000 offerings of ghee-Payasam to the Lord with the mantra "Namo astu nilagrivaya"—1,000 karshapanas (gold/silver coins) will be produced. Another: With the mantra "Pramuncha dhanvanah" according to the rules, sacrifice 1,000 pieces of food in the kitchen; the food will become inexhaustible. Another: With "Namo vah kirikebhyah", sacrifice 10,000 sesame seeds; 1,000 karshapanas will be produced. (From the Kalika Purana).

[For wealth]:
Shaunaka says: With the mantra "Asau yastamrah" and the following part, worship the Sun at both sunrise and sunset; he obtains inexhaustible wealth.

[Bodhayana says]:
Chanting any of the Rudra, Maharudra, or Atirudra sequences, sacrifice Udumbara sticks or honey-mixed unbroken rice in the mentioned number; wealth will be produced.

[For rain]:
Shaunaka says: With "Asau yastamrah", living on water, vegetables, and barley, sacrifice 10,000 Vetasa sticks, or 10,000 sticks smeared with goat's milk; the Lord Sun will send rain.

[For long life]:
He says: One desiring long life, having worshipped the Lord according to the rules, and having offered red, yellow, and black sacrifices in all directions—chant the Rudra-Ekadashini eleven times, perform the sixteen-fold worship with "Arat te goghna", and chant the same mantra 1,000 times. If sick, give Dakshina to a Brahmin on the altar-day and have him chant 1,000 times; he will be freed from diseases and live for 100 years.

[For a son]:
Shaunaka says: With "Pari no rudrasya", sacrifice 10,000 Banyan sticks; a long-lived son will be born.
[For cattle]:
With "Namo vah kirikebhyah", sacrifice 10,000 Vakankata and Udumbara sticks smeared with curd; he becomes the owner of infinite cattle. || 1 ||

[Na. Pa. A.] Rudradhyaya | 129

(Cattle ritual continued): And his cows will not perish. Or, with the same (sixth mantra of 11th Anuvaka), sacrifice 100 offerings of ghee and 11 offerings of cow-milk; eleven cows will be obtained.

[For power of speech]:
Shaunaka says: Establish a Linga with anthill-clay at a great crossroads and bathe it with Panchagavya. Enchanting it with "Namo vah kirikebhyah", offer ghee-Payasam for two days with the mantra "Ma nastoke". In front of Dakshinamurti, take the powder of Vacha fruit and nutmeg, place it in a copper vessel, mix with cow-ghee, enchant it with 10,000 repetitions using the index finger, and consume it; his words will never be refuted.

Again: With "Drape andhasaspate" etc., sacrifice 100,000 times; his clothes will become imperishable.
[For control (vashya)]:
Bodhayana says: Chanting any of the Rudra sequences, sacrifice flowers; he brings the whole world under control. If he desires to make the whole world follow his will, he should stand in navel-deep water and chant the Rudras. Having worshipped Shambhu and taken a vow, facing the Sun, he should always repeat the Rudras. For the wise one who repeats the Rudras thus, controlling the senses, Shiva (the Bull-flagged one) appears as the bestower of boons, being fond of devotees.

[For health]:
Shaunaka says: With "Ma no mahantam", sacrifice 10,000 sesame seeds; children and servants will have health. In case of impending death or all fears, one desiring immediate liberation—having performed nyasa as before and worshipping with "Midhushtama" and sixteen-fold rituals, chant the Rudra-Ekadashini eleven times or have a knower of Rudra chant it; he is freed from all fears without doubt. With "Imam rudraya", sacrifice 10,000 each of curd, honey, and Payasam; all fears will be pacified.

[For peace]:
One desiring peace, having performed nyasa as before and worshipped the Lord, choose a Rudra-knowing Brahmin as Acharya. Approach a Svayambhu Linga and worship it there also with the same procedure. Bathe with five nectars (Panchamrita), perform Homa with ghee using the first Anuvaka, worship the Lord, and chant the eleven Anuvakas beginning with "Namaste rudra manyave". || 17 ||

130 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Pa. A.]

(Peace ritual continued): Similarly, at the end of the chant, chant each of the eleven Anuvakas beginning with "Agnavishnu sajoshasa". Bathe again with Panchamrita, worship the Lord, offer food and eatables, and worship four full pots (kalasha) containing five types of mud, five types of leaves, and five gems, using unbroken rice, scent, incense, etc. Wrap them in white cloth and, thinking of peace, chant "Sham no vatah pavatam" and perform the Abhisheka; peace results. Then he becomes healthy. Give a pair of white clothes, a cow, and gold to the Acharya, and feed Brahmins; peace results.

Shaunaka again: With "Ma no mahantam", sacrifice 10,000 Ashvattha sticks. Every year during a journey, sacrifice 10,000. By this, there is peace for children, women, and the elderly. With "Namo bhavaya" and "Namo jyeshthaya", enchant ash and protect those afflicted by spirits (Kumaragraha etc.). Tie a knot in the hair at night; there will be no fear from spirits. With "Midhushtama shivatama", in case of great fear, fear of thieves, fire, or danger to life, chant mentally; he will return safely. With "Imam rudraya", sacrifice 100,000 golden sprouts; even one whose life is ebbing due to great fever will live long. With "Namaste rudra manyave" and the next three Riks, sacrifice 10,000 each of barley, wheat, Gavidhuka, and wild sesame smeared with curd, honey, and ghee; this pacifies the wind-disorders (Vayu) within eleven or twelve nights. With "Adhyavochadadhivakta", perform protection for all. For the sick, enchant mustard seeds seven times and throw them in the four directions; protection is done in the ten directions. Peace results for one going on a path. Through mustard seeds, he is liberated from Brahma-rakshasas, ghosts, spirits, etc. With the final Rik of "Ya te rudra shiva tanuh", enchant 1,000 times daily and tie eleven knots in a thread for children and pregnant women; they will flourish happily.

[Expelling demons]:
With "Sahasrani...", wearing red clothes and red turban, and anointing with red paste, worship the Lord. In a cemetery, on a river bank, at a crossroads, or in a forest—having made an image of the enemy or demon out of barley flour enchanted eleven times, wrapped in cotton and rice and smeared with oil—place it in the cemetery fire. Anoint the chest of the image with bitter oil, establish it, and pierce the eleven vital spots with eleven arrows in the southern direction. This expels the enemy from the neighborhood or the entire world. With "Namo rudrebhyah", sacrifice in a thornless fire at midday; spirits, demons, and pishachas will fall away from the nation, and sky-roaming sorceresses or yakshas will be turned to ash.

[More pacification rituals]:
With "Imam rudraya", in case of disease among cows, light a fire in the middle of the cowshed and sacrifice 10,000 Vakankata sticks; cows will be saved. For all quadrupeds, perform the same ritual. || 1 ||

[Na. Pa. A.] Rudradhyaya 131

(Pacification continued): Similarly, with "Namaste rudra manyave", if cows are afflicted, light a fire in the cowshed and sacrifice 10,000 Vakankata sticks. By doubling the ritual for animals, great peace is achieved.

[Ritual for killing/destroying an enemy]:
One desiring the destruction of an enemy, having performed nyasa as before and offered red-rice sacrifice to the direction-guardians—performing sixteen-fold worship with "Stuhi shrutam", chant the Rudra-Ekadashini eleven times. Then chant "Stuhi shrutam" 1,000 times daily. Within seven nights, the enemy is destroyed. One desiring to create a 'Kritya' (magical entity) should worship the deity wearing red clothes, red turban, and red garlands. In a cemetery, sacrifice 100,000 small red sticks smeared with thick oil. "Arise, red-bodied, red-eyed, red-haired girl!"—quickly satisfy her with a pot of blood and flesh. Otherwise, she might harm the performer. She, being ready, will ask "Whom shall I crush? Tell me, and if he is in the three worlds, I will destroy him." This ritual is for a single body, a village, a city, or a house. (Detailed instructions on fasting, vows like Tula-purusha, and chanting follow).

With "Pramuncha dhanvanah" and the next six Riks, enchant the weapons; enemies brought to battle will flee. They will not stand in front. Sacrifice 10,000 thorny sticks. If a powerful enemy from the Kshatriya class etc. appears—for a Kshatriya, sacrifice for 1/3 of the day; for a Vaishya, 1/6; for a Shudra, 1/4. Thus the work is unhindered. With "Vikikirida vilohita", sacrifice in the mouth of a sleeping enemy after many rounds; all will be confused and flee. With "Namo hiranyabahave" Anuvaka, sacrifice mustard seeds smeared with bitter oil for fear of a king; the whole circle of enemies is expelled. With the sound 'Phat', hang a garland upside down on the Lord, use a blood-smeared cloth with Trikatu powder, and using the Lord's hand-finger to draw lines, chant the enemy's name; the enemy with his army will be expelled. Thus an entire village can be expelled in one day. A king is finished in a day and night, a Kshatriya in half a day, a Vaishya in 1/3, and a Shudra in 1/4. This is stated by Shaunaka in Hemadri.

[From the Meru Tantra]:
"Then perform the 'Raudri Dhara' (continuous stream). Its procedure is stated: With the two chapters 'Namaste rudra' and 'Agnavishnu'. This 'Avartana' (repetition) is said to give the fruit of bathing in the Ganga. The man who performs this Abhisheka daily— || 1 ||

132 Sayanacharya—along with Bhatta Bhaskara Bhashya— [Na. Pa. A.]

(Meru Tantra continued):
—His sins born of that day and divine sins will be destroyed. By Sandhyavandana they are weakened, and the rest from previous births will follow. One reading of 'Namaste rudra' (Namakam) and its subsequent Anuvakas, then 'Bhavaya namah' etc., then 'Agnavishnu' (Chamakam)—this is called 'Rudri', the cutter of fierce sins. He who performs the continuous Abhisheka with this daily—sins of many births perish within a year. Eleven 'Rudris' are called a 'Laghu-Rudra'. Those whose Linga is anointed with this will never see the Sun's son (Yama/Death). Eleven of these (Laghu-Rudras) are called a 'Maha-Rudra'. The poor obtain wealth, and it destroys great sins. A Homa performed with this grants the fruit of a Soma-sacrifice. For those not eligible for initiation, this is the rule for three fortnights. Eleven 'Maha-Rudras' are called an 'Atirudra'. Since it removes extreme sins, no other atonement is seen like it."
Thus concludes the procedure stated in Meru Tantra.

Thus ends the Fifth Prapathaka named 'Shri Rudra Prashna' in the Fire-section (Agni-kanda) of the Yajurveda Bhashya composed by Shri Bhatta Bhaskara.
|| Thus ends the Bhashya on the Namakam ||

rudrabhashya.sanskrit:
---
१५६                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।
---

पञ्चसहस्रजपातुरञ्चरणसिद्धिः । अन्यतस्सर्वेम् 'सहस्राणि
सहस्रशः' इत्येकोक्तम् ॥

    य ग्तावन्तश्च सूर्याꣳसश्च दिशो
रुद्रा वितस्थिरे । तेषाꣳ सहस्रयोजनेऽव
धन्वानि तन्मसि ॥

    अथ दशमः— य एतावन्त इति । 'सहस्राणि सह-
स्रशः' इत्यारभ्य यावन्तोऽमी रुद्रा उक्ताः एतावन्तश्च, इतो-
ऽपि सूर्यासः बहुतराः इयत्तामवीळ्य वर्तमानाः ये रुद्राः
दिशो रुद्रा वितस्थिरे सर्वां दिशो व्याप्य स्थिताः, तेषां
सहस्रयोजनेऽव धन्वानि तन्मसीति । अत्र ऋष्यादिकं
पूर्ववत् ॥

    नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां येऽन्तरिक्षे
ये दिवि येषामन्नं वातो वर्षिमषवस्ते-
भ्यो दश प्राचींदेश दक्षिणा दश प्रती-
चींदेशोदीचींदेशोध्वाꣳस्तेभ्यो नमस्ते नो
मृडयन्तु ते यं भिऋमो यञ्च नो देछि तं
वो जन्मे द्धामि ॥

    अथ 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां' इत्यादिभिर्मन्त्रैः
पृथिव्यन्तरिक्षुलोकास्थितान् रुद्राञ्मस्कृत्य स्वस्य सुखं क्षे-
विषणां दु:खं च प्रार्थयते । अग्रेऽथं मन्त्रत्रयविभागः— 'नमो
रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां येषामन्निषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदे-
श....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये अन्तरिक्षे
येषां बात इषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इति द्वि-
तीयो मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये दिवि येषां वर्षिमषवः
तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । तत्र
प्रथमखायसर्थः— नमो रुद्रेभ्यो अस्तु ये पृथिव्यां वर्त-
न्ते, येषां च अन्नम् इषवः इषुस्तानीयं हिंसायाचवनम्;
अपध्यानभक्षणम् अतिमात्राशनं च कारयित्वा तेन रोगा-
तुल्तय काञ्चिद्धाघन्ते, काञ्चिद्धनविघातेन क्षुधमुल्तय पीड-
यन्ति, काञ्चिद्धनार्थे चौर्यादि कारयित्वा वधाधिकं प्राणयन्ति;
एवं पापिष्ठहिंसायाम् अञ्चम् निमित्तामिति तदेव एषामिषवः ।
तेभ्यो रुद्रेभ्यो दश भाचीः प्राञ्जुखाखिलरूपा अञ्जुलीः
करोमि । प्राञ्जुखत्वेन अञ्जलीकरणे हि दशाञ्जुलयः प्रागभा-
भवन्ति । एवं दक्षिणाभुभ्वान्तेषु दृष्टव्याः । ईदशाखिलोरू-
पाञ्जुल्युपलाखिताञ्जमस्कारान्करोमील्यर्थः । अनेन कायकन-
मस्कारो दर्शितः, तेभ्यो नम इति मानसः, नमो रुद्रेभ्यो

---
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                    १५७
---

    अथ 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्याम्' इत्यादिभिर्मन्त्रैः
पृथिव्यन्तरिक्षुलोकास्थितान् रुद्राञ्मस्कृत्य स्वस्य सुखं क्षे-
विषणां दु:खं च प्रार्थयते । अग्रेथं मन्त्रत्रयविभागः— 'नमो
रुद्रेभ्यो ये पृथिव्यां येषामन्निषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश-
श....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये अन्तरिक्षे
येषां बात इषवः तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इति द्वि-
तीयो मन्त्रः । 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यो ये दिवि येषां वर्षिमषवः
तेभ्यो दश प्राचींदेश....द्धामि' इत्येको मन्त्रः । तत्र
प्रथमखायसर्थः— नमो रुद्रेभ्यो अस्तु ये पृथिव्यां वर्त-
न्ते, येषां च अन्नम् इषवः इषुस्तानीयं हिंसायाचवनम्;
अपध्यानभक्षणम् अतिमात्राशनं च कारयित्वा तेन रोगा-
तुल्तय कांचिद्धाघन्ते, कांचिद्धनविघातेन क्षुधमुल्तय पीड-
यन्ति, कांचिद्धनार्थे चौर्यादि कारयित्वा वधाधिकं प्राणयन्ति;
एवं पापिष्ठहिंसायाम् अञ्चम् निमित्तामिति तदेव एषामिषवः ।
तेभ्यो रुद्रेभ्यो दश प्राचीः प्राञ्जुखाखिलरूपा अञ्जुलीः
करोमि । प्राञ्जुखत्वेन अञ्जलीकरणे हि दशाञ्जुलयः प्रागभा-
भवन्ति । एवं दक्षिणाभुभ्वान्तेषु दृष्टव्याः । ईदशाखिलोरू-
पाञ्जुल्युपलाखिताञ्जमस्कारान्करोमील्यर्थः । अनेन कायकन-
मस्कारो दर्शितः, तेभ्यो नम इति मानसः, नमो रुद्रेभ्यो

---
१५८                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।
---

इति मन्त्रस्यादौ यो नमस्कारः स वाचिकः । एवं त्रिकरणैर्-
नमस्कुर्वाणाः वयं यं द्विष्मः यश्च नो अस्मान् द्वेष्टि, तमु
अस्मत्पापतल्फलतदुपकरणरूपं द्वेषिणं हे रुद्राः, वः युष्माकं
जघ्मे विदारितास्ये द्धामि स्थापयामि; तं भञ्जयेत्यर्थः ।
एवं द्वितीयत्तृतीययोरपि योजनीयम् । ये अन्तरिक्षे रुद्रा व-
र्तन्ते येषां बात इषवः वाणाः, वायुविकाराभ्युत्किविविधरोगो-
त्पादनेन तीव्रतरवायूत्पादनादिभिश्च पापिनो हिंसन्तीत्यर्थः ।
ये दिवि रुद्राः दिवि वर्तन्ते येषां वर्षिमषवः अतिवृष्टप्तारु-
ष्ठयादिभिः हिंसन्तीत्यर्थः, शेषं पूर्वविदित सर्वे रमणी-
यम् । अत्र 'नमो रुद्रेभ्यः' इत्यादि समाप्यन्तं यजु-
ञ्चयमेको मन्त्रः । अत्र देवता ऋषिश्च रुद्र एव । ध्यानं
तु— 'सहस्राणि सहस्रशः' इत्यत्र उक्तमेव प्राह्यम् । अञ्च-
मस्यदिप्रदोऽयं मन्त्रः । पुरञ्चरणादिप्रकारः, कृत्योत्पादनशा-
तिष्मरतलादिफलभेदाञ्च रुद्रकल्पादिप्रपञ्चिता दृष्टव्याः ।।

    त्र्यंबकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धे-
नम् । उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय
माऽमृतात् । यो रुद्रो अग्नौ यो अप्सु य
ओषधीषु यो रुद्रो विश्वा भुवना बिवेश
तस्मै रुद्राय नमो अस्तु ।तस्मु स्तुहि यः खि-

---
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।                    १५९
---

षुः सुघन्वा यो विश्वस्य क्षयति भेषजस्य ।
यक्ष्वामहे सौमनसाय रुद्रं नमोभिर्देवम-
घरं दुवस्य । अयं मे अस्तु भगवानयं मे
भगवत्तः । अयं मे विश्वमेषजोयꣳ
शियाभिमर्शनः । ये ते सहस्रमꣳ ता पा-
शा मृत्यो मर्त्याय हन्तवे । तान्यञ्छ्य
मायया सर्वानव यजामहे । मृत्यवे खा-
हा मृत्यवे स्वाहा । ओं नमो भगवते
रुद्राय विष्णवे मृत्युमें पाहि ॥

    इति श्रीमत्परमहंसपरिव्राजकसार्वभोम
श्रीमद्वैतविद्याप्रतिष्ठापक
श्रीमदभिनवशंकरभगवता कृतं
श्रीरुद्रभाष्यं संपूर्णम् ॥

rudrabhashya.english:
---
156                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

— the "namaḥ" (salutation) at the beginning of the mantra is the verbal salutation (vācika). Thus we who bow with all three faculties (body, mind, and speech) — that which we hate (yaṃ dviṣmaḥ) and that which hates us (yaś ca no dveṣṭi) — that enemy, who embodies our sinful deeds and their fruits and the instruments thereof — O Rudras, into your open mouth (jaghme, the open jaws) we place (dadhāmi) him — meaning: destroy him. The same connection applies to the second and third mantras. The Rudras who dwell in the atmosphere (antarikṣe) — whose arrows are wind (vāta, their arrow being a weapon of affliction): they afflict sinners by producing various diseases through wind-disorders, and through generating violent storms and the like — this is the meaning. The Rudras who dwell in the heavens (divi) — whose arrows are rain (varṣima, rain-related) — by excessive rainfall, drought, and the like they afflict sinners — the rest is to be understood everywhere as it pleases. The entire collection of yajus (Yajurvedic verses) beginning with "namo rudrabhyaḥ" forms a single mantra. Here the deity and seer are both Rudra. The meditation verse is the same as what was stated under "sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ" — that is to be used here as well. This mantra is primarily for warding off evil influences and harmful acts (añcamasya-di-prada). The method of puraścaraṇa (ritual practice for mantra-perfection), the variations in the results such as neutralizing hostile acts (kṛtyotpādana), pacification (śānti), subjugation (stambhana), and the like — these are to be found elaborated in the Rudra-Kalpa and other texts.

    tryambakaṃ yajāmahe sugandhinṃ puṣṭi-vardhe-
    nam | urvārukam iva bandhanān mṛtyor mukṣīya
    mā 'mṛtāt | yo rudro agnau yo apsu ya
    oṣadhīṣu yo rudro viśvā bhuvanā viveśa
    tasmai rudrāya namo astu | tasmai stuhi yaḥ khi-

---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    157
---

    ṣuḥ sughanvā yo viśvasya kṣayati bheṣajasya |
    yakṣvāmahe saumanasāya rudraṃ namobhir devam-
    agharaṃ duvasya | ayam me astu bhagavān ayam me
    bhagavatīdaḥ | ayam me viśvam eṣajo 'yaṃ
    śivābhimarśanaḥ | ye te sahasramanta pā-
    śā mṛtyo marttyāya hantave | tāny añjhya
    māyayā sarvān ava yajāmahe | mṛtyave svā-
    hā mṛtyave svāhā | oṃ namo bhagavate
    rudrāya viṣṇave mṛtyumme pāhi ||

    We worship the three-eyed one (tryambakam), the fragrant one (sugandhinm),
    the nourisher of all (puṣṭi-vardhenam).
    Like the cucumber freed from its vine — may I be freed from death (mṛtyu),
    not from immortality (amṛta).
    He who is Rudra in fire, who is in the waters,
    who is in the herbs and plants — Rudra who has entered all the worlds —
    to that Rudra, salutation.
    Praise him who is swift-arrowed and beautifully armed,
    who is the lord of all medicine and healing.
    We worship with salutations Rudra the gracious god,
    the sinless one, that we may attain his benevolence.
    May this blessed one be mine; may this one be my bounty.
    May this one be my universal healer,
    may this one touch me with his auspicious hand.
    O Death (mṛtyo), those thousands of snares (pāśā) of yours
    set out to strike down the mortal —
    all of those we worship away with sacred rites, by your māyā (divine power).
    Svāhā to Death! Svāhā to Death!
    Oṃ. Salutation to the Blessed Lord Rudra-Viṣṇu — protect me from death.

    Thus is complete the Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam (Commentary on Śrī Rudra),
    composed by the most venerable Śrīmat Paramahaṃsa Parivrājaka Sārvabhauma
    (the supreme wandering renunciant),
    the establisher of the Advaita (non-dual) Vidyā (wisdom),
    the revered Abhinava Śaṃkara Bhagavatā.

[decorative lotus symbol]

---
158                    Sri Rudra Bhashyam
---

— the "namaḥ" (salutation) at the beginning of the mantra is the verbal salutation (vācika, expressed through speech). Thus we who bow with all three faculties (body, mind, and speech) — that which we hate (yaṃ dviṣmaḥ) and that which hates us (yaś ca no dveṣṭi) — that enemy, who embodies our sinful karmas, their fruits, and the instruments thereof — O Rudras, into your open jaws (jaghme, the gaping mouth) we place him (dadhāmi) — meaning: destroy him. The same connection (yojanā) applies to the second and third mantras. The Rudras who dwell in the atmosphere (antarikṣe) — whose arrows are wind (vāta): they afflict sinners by producing various diseases through disorders of wind (vāyu-vikāra), and through generating extremely violent storms and the like — this is the meaning. The Rudras who dwell in the heavens (divi) — whose arrows are rain (varṣima-iṣavaḥ): by excessive rainfall, drought, and the like they afflict sinners — the rest is to be understood in a pleasing manner throughout.

The entire collection of yajus-verses (Yajurveda mantras) beginning with "namo rudrabhyaḥ" through to the end forms a single mantra. Here the deity and seer are both Rudra. As for the meditation verse — the one stated under "sahasrāṇi sahasraśaḥ" is to be used here as well. This mantra is primarily for the granting of good fortune and for warding off evil influences. The method of puraścaraṇa (the ritual procedure for achieving mantra-siddhi, perfection in the mantra) — including the various fruits such as neutralizing hostile acts (kṛtya-utpādana), pacification (śānti), subjugation (stambhana), and so forth — these are elaborated in the Rudra-Kalpa (Rudra ritual manual) and other texts, and are to be consulted there.

    nam | urvārukam iva bandhanān mṛtyor mukṣīya
    mā 'mṛtāt | yo rudro agnau yo apsu ya
    oṣadhīṣu yo rudro viśvā bhuvanā viveśa
    tasmai rudrāya namo astu | tasmai stuhi yaḥ khi-

---
Sri Rudra Bhashyam                    159
---

    ṣuḥ sughanvā yo viśvasya kṣayati bheṣajasya |
    yakṣvāmahe saumanasāya rudraṃ namobhir devam-
    agharaṃ duvasya | ayam me astu bhagavān ayam me
    bhagavatīdaḥ | ayam me viśvam eṣajo 'yaṃ
    śivābhimarśanaḥ | ye te sahasramanta pā-
    śā mṛtyo marttyāya hantave | tāny añjhya
    māyayā sarvān ava yajāmahe | mṛtyave svā-
    hā mṛtyave svāhā | oṃ namo bhagavate
    rudrāya viṣṇave mṛtyumme pāhi ||

    We worship the three-eyed one (tryambakam), the supremely fragrant one,
    the nourisher of all beings (puṣṭi-vardhenam).
    Like a cucumber freed from its vine — may I be freed from death,
    not from immortality.
    He who is Rudra in fire, who is in the waters,
    who is in the herbs and plants — Rudra who has entered all the worlds —
    to that Rudra, salutation.
    Praise him who is swift with arrows and of beautiful bow (su-ghanvā),
    who is the lord and sustainer of all medicine and healing.
    We worship with salutations Rudra the benevolent god,
    the sinless one, that we may attain his gracious favor (saumanasā).
    May this blessed Lord be mine; may this one be my source of bounty.
    May this one be my universal healer (viśva-eṣaja),
    may this one touch me with his auspicious hand (śivābhimarśana).
    O Death, those thousands of nooses (pāśāḥ) of yours
    laid out to strike down the mortal —
    all of those we worship away through sacred rites and divine power (māyā).
    Svāhā to Death! Svāhā to Death!
    Oṃ. Salutation to the Blessed Lord Rudra-Viṣṇu — protect me from death.

    Thus is the Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam (Commentary on the Śrī Rudra Hymn) complete,
    composed by the most venerable Śrīmat Paramahaṃsa Parivrājaka Sārvabhauma
    (the supreme wandering renunciant of the highest order),
    the establisher of the Advaita (non-dual) Vidyā (sacred wisdom),
    the revered Abhinava Śaṃkara Bhagavatā.
